schema.title: GMX Protected Character Count
schema.description: An accumulation of the character count for text that has been marked as 'protected', or otherwise not translatable (XLIFF text enclosed in <mrk mtype="protected"> elements). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

toolMeta.description: An accumulation of the character count for text that has been marked as 'protected', or otherwise not translatable (XLIFF text enclosed in <mrk mtype="protected"> elements). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: Tallies the [GMX-V 2.0](http://www.xtm-intl.com/manuals/gmx-v/GMX-V-2.0.html) `ProtectedCharacterCount` category — the character count for text that has been marked as protected, or otherwise not translatable (for example XLIFF text enclosed in `<mrk mtype="protected">` elements). Unlike the match-based GMX steps, this step counts only the units flagged as non-translatable, counting characters in their source; for translatable units it removes any previously recorded protected metric. Characters are counted in canonical form — whitespace and punctuation are excluded (hyphens and apostrophes inside words are kept) — and stored as GMX-V metrics annotations for downstream consumption (for example by the Scoping Report Step). This is the character-count counterpart of the protected word-count step.
processingNotes: Counts only text units marked non-translatable; this is the one GMX count step that does not restrict itself to translatable units.
For translatable units the step actively removes any existing protected metric from the segment, source, and text unit.
Character counting excludes whitespace and punctuation per GMX-V, but retains hyphens and apostrophes that occur inside a word.

entries.description: Translate block content using Google Translate.

schema.title: Google Translate Translate
schema.description: Translate content using Google Translate

toolMeta.description: Translate content using Google Translate

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: API key for the MT provider

email.title: Email
email.description: Account email for higher rate limits (mymemory)

doc.overview:
The **Google Translate** tool translates untranslated block content with the
Google Cloud Translation API. The provider is fixed by the tool name, so there
is no provider field. A target language is required and a Google API key must
be supplied (resolved from the OS keychain by the CLI, or set inline); a Google
Cloud project ID may also be provided.

Blocks that carry inline codes are round-tripped through semantic HTML so the
API preserves the tags natively, then reconstructed from the response. With a
session cache, targets already translated in a previous run are reused, so
repeat runs skip blocks that are already done. When constructed without
credentials the tool falls back to an offline demo provider so it remains
listable and runnable for illustration.

apiKey.description: Google Cloud API key.

email.help: Part of the shared MT credential schema; consumed only by the MyMemory tool.

projectId.help: The optional Google Cloud project ID associated with the API key.

region.help: Part of the shared MT credential schema; consumed only by the Microsoft Translator tool.

subscriptionKey.help: Part of the shared MT credential schema; consumed only by the Microsoft Translator tool.

limitations: Requires a target language and a Google API key; without a key the tool runs the offline demo provider, not Google Translate.
Google makes a network round-trip per block; cost and latency scale with content volume.
The provider is fixed to Google Translate by the tool name and cannot be changed through configuration.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks with non-empty source; other parts pass through unchanged.
Blocks with inline codes round-trip through semantic HTML so tags survive translation.
With a session cache, already-translated targets are reused and fresh translations are cached.

examples.title: Translate with Google
examples.description: Translate untranslated blocks using a Google Cloud API key.

entries.description: Align text units in two id-based files (e.g. Java properties). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Id-Based Aligner
schema.description: Align text units in two id-based files (e.g. Java properties). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

generateTMX.title: Generate a TMX file:
generateTMX.description: If generateTMX is false generate bilingual TextUnits, otherwise (true) output a TMX file

tmxOutputPath.title: TMX output path
tmxOutputPath.description: Full path of the output TMX file

replaceWithSource.title: Fall back to source text
replaceWithSource.description: If no target text is available, use the source text

copyToTarget.title: Copy to/over the target (WARNING: Copied target will not be segmented)
copyToTarget.description: Copy to/over the target (an annotation will still be created if requested). WARNING: Copied target will not be segmented and any exisiting target will be lost.

storeAsAltTranslation.title: Create an alternate translation annotation
storeAsAltTranslation.description: Store the matched target as an alternate translation so that subsequent steps can see it.

suppressTusWithNoTarget.title: Suppress TUs with no target
suppressTusWithNoTarget.description: Do not pass on any TUs for which no targets could be found.

useTextUnitIds.title: Align based on TextUnit IDs
useTextUnitIds.description: Align based on TextUnit IDs, rather than resource names.

toolMeta.description: Align text units in two id-based files (e.g. Java properties). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: Aligns text units from two input files by matching their ids (taken from `TextUnit.getName()`). Works with any filter that produces unique names for its text units, such as the Properties Filter. Can either produce aligned bilingual text units in the pipeline or generate a TMX file as output.

copyToTarget.help:
Copies the aligned target content directly onto the text unit's target. **This is destructive** — any existing target content is overwritten and the copied target will **not** be segmented.

If the entry from the target file is marked as *approved*, the approved property is preserved and passed along.

Required when generating TMX if you want target `<tuv>` elements in the output.
notes: **WARNING**: Copied target will not be segmented and any existing target content will be lost.
An alternate translation annotation is still created if that option is also enabled.

generateTMX.help:
Controls the step's output mode:

- **Checked**: produces a TMX file at the specified output path. The filter events passed through the pipeline are **unchanged** — alignment results go only to the TMX file.
- **Unchecked**: each text unit in the returned events is replaced with a **new bilingual text unit** containing aligned source and target content.

**Note**: for target `<tuv>` data to appear in the generated TMX, the **Copy to/over the target** option must also be checked.
notes: When generating TMX, the pipeline events are passed through unchanged — downstream steps will not see aligned content unless you also enable **Copy to/over the target**.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for target <tuv> data to appear in the generated TMX

replaceWithSource.help: When a source text unit has no matching target, the source text is used as the target content instead. Useful for creating complete bilingual files where missing translations fall back to the original language.

storeAsAltTranslation.help: Attaches the matched target as an `AltTranslation` annotation on the text unit. This makes the aligned target visible to subsequent pipeline steps (e.g., leveraging steps, translation memory steps) without overwriting the text unit's actual target.

suppressTusWithNoTarget.help:
Filters out text units that have no matching target from the pipeline output. Useful when downstream steps should only process fully aligned entries.

Without this option, unmatched source text units generate a warning but are still passed through.

tmxOutputPath.help: Full path for the generated TMX file. Only used when **Generate a TMX file** is enabled.
tmxOutputPath.values: A file system path ending in `.tmx`. Can be absolute or relative to the pipeline working directory.
examples: `aligned.tmx` — default, writes to the current working directory

useTextUnitIds.help: Switches the matching key from `TextUnit.getName()` (resource name) to `TextUnit.getId()` (text unit ID). Use this when the filter produces meaningful IDs rather than names for matching.

limitations: Assumes each text unit has a **unique** name (or ID) value. Filters that produce duplicate names will cause incorrect alignments.
Aligns at the **text unit level only** — segments within text units are not individually aligned.
Both input files are expected to be **non-segmented**.
For multilingual target files, alignment requires both matching ID **and** matching source text — if source texts differ, no alignment is made.
processingNotes: The **source** file is the first (primary) input and provides source content. The **target** file is the second input and provides target content.
If the target file is **monolingual** (e.g., Java properties), the source extracted from that file is used as target content.
If the target file is **multilingual** (e.g., XLIFF), the actual target extracted from that file is used as target content, and an additional source-text match check is performed.
Text units present in the source but missing from the target generate a **warning**. Text units in the target but not in the source are **silently ignored**.

examples.title: Align two properties files into bilingual text units
examples.description: Aligns a source English `.properties` file with a target French `.properties` file, producing bilingual text units in the pipeline. Missing targets fall back to source text.
examples.config:
```yaml
generateTMX: false
replaceWithSource: true
storeAsAltTranslation: true
copyToTarget: false
```

examples.title: Generate a TMX from two properties files
examples.description: Produces a TMX file from aligned properties files. Both `generateTMX` and `copyToTarget` must be enabled for target `<tuv>` data to appear in the TMX output.
examples.config:
```yaml
generateTMX: true
tmxOutputPath: /output/en-fr-aligned.tmx
copyToTarget: true
storeAsAltTranslation: true
```

entries.description:
Copies the source text of the second input into the target of the first input based on matching id.
Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Id-Based Copy
schema.description:
Copies the source text of the second input into the target of the first input based on matching id.
Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

markAsTranslateNo.title: Set the text unit as non-translatable
markAsTranslateNo.description: When enabled, matched text units in the destination file are marked as non-translatable after the reference text is copied.

markAsApproved.title: Set the target property 'approved' to 'yes'
markAsApproved.description: When enabled, the 'approved' property of matched target entries is set to 'yes' after copying.

toolMeta.description:
Copies the source text of the second input into the target of the first input based on matching id.
Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: Copies text from a reference file (second input) into a destination file (first input) by matching text unit ids (`TextUnit.getName()`). When a match is found, monolingual reference sources are copied to the destination's target; for multilingual references, the target text is used. Unmatched entries in the destination file remain untouched. Works with any filter that produces unique names for text units, such as the Properties Filter.

markAsApproved.description: When enabled, the 'approved' property of matched target entries is set to 'yes' after copying.
markAsApproved.help:
When a text unit in the destination file matches an entry in the reference file, this sets the target's `approved` property to `yes` after copying.

This is useful when the reference file represents reviewed/approved translations and you want the destination to reflect that approval status.

Entries that do **not** have a match retain their original `approved` state.
notes: Only applies to matched entries. Unmatched entries keep their original properties.

markAsTranslateNo.description: When enabled, matched text units in the destination file are marked as non-translatable after the reference text is copied.
markAsTranslateNo.help:
When a text unit in the destination file matches an entry in the reference file, this marks the text unit as **non-translatable** after copying.

Entries that do **not** have a match retain their original translatable/non-translatable state.

> Note: Text units already marked as non-translatable in the destination file are **skipped entirely** — they are not modified even if a matching entry exists in the reference file.
notes: Only applies to matched entries. Unmatched entries keep their original properties.

limitations: Uses an in-memory table for the reference file — may hit memory limits with very large reference files.
No adjustment is made for inline codes when copying content between different file formats — results depend on whether inline codes exist and the formats involved.
Copies complete text unit content with no adjustment for segmented entries. Use the Desegmentation Step beforehand if needed.
No warning is generated when an entry in the destination file has no match in the reference file.
processingNotes: The reference file (second input) is read entirely into a table indexed by text unit name before processing the destination file.
For **monolingual** reference files (e.g., properties), the **source** text is copied into the destination's target. For **multilingual** reference files (e.g., PO), the **target** text is used.
Text units marked as non-translatable in the destination file are never modified, even if a match exists.
After each pair is processed, unused reference entries are listed as warnings — these may simply indicate obsolete entries removed from the newer file.
Both files must have text units with **unique** names (e.g., unique `resname` in XLIFF).
If a destination file has no associated reference file, a warning is generated and the file is treated as having an empty reference.

examples.title: Aligning properties files
examples.description: Copy translations from a reference properties file into a destination properties file, marking matched entries as approved. Both files must have matching keys (text unit names). The reference file is Input List 2 and the destination is Input List 1.

entries.description: Create a modified copy of image files. Expects: image raw document. Sends back: image raw document.

schema.title: Image Modification
schema.description: Create a modified copy of image files. Expects: image raw document. Sends back: image raw document.

makeGray.title: Convert to gray scale
makeGray.description: Convert the image colors into a set of grays.

toolMeta.description: Create a modified copy of image files. Expects: image raw document. Sends back: image raw document.

doc.overview: This step modifies the size or format of image files. It takes a raw document containing an image and sends back a modified raw document. Supports scaling width and height independently, converting to grayscale, and changing the output image format.

format.help: Sets the image format for the output file. Leave empty (or use `<same as the original>`) to keep the original format. If the original format is not supported for output, a warning is generated and the output falls back to **PNG** if possible.
notes: Selecting a specific output format does **not** automatically change the file extension — you may need a separate renaming step.
If the original format is unsupported for writing, the step falls back to PNG output with a warning.
examples: Empty string — keep the original format

makeGray.description: Convert the image colors into a set of grays.
makeGray.help: Converts all colors in the image to a corresponding set of grays, producing a grayscale output image.

scaleHeight.help: Sets the height of the output image as a percentage of the original. Use `100` for the same height as the original, `50` for half-height, `200` for double-height.
scaleHeight.values: Integer greater than 0. Value of `100` means no change.

scaleWidth.help: Sets the width of the output image as a percentage of the original. Use `100` for the same width as the original, `50` for half-width, `200` for double-width.
scaleWidth.values: Integer greater than 0. Value of `100` means no change.

limitations: Supported input image formats depend on the Java VM, its version, and the platform — not all formats may be available in all environments.
Changing the output format does **not** automatically rename the file extension.
processingNotes: Width and height scaling are independent — non-uniform scaling is possible (e.g., 50% width with 100% height).
The step operates on raw documents, so it must be placed in a pipeline path that routes image files as raw documents.

examples.title: Scale images to half size
examples.description: Reduces both width and height to 50% of the original, keeping the original format.

examples.title: Convert to grayscale PNG
examples.description: Converts images to grayscale and outputs as PNG format.
examples.config:
```yaml
scaleWidth: 100
scaleHeight: 100
format: png
makeGray: true
```

entries.description: Flag identical source strings translated differently, or different sources sharing one translation.

checkPerFile.title: Per-File Checking
checkPerFile.description: Process each input file individually instead of comparing segments across all documents

checkSourceInconsistency.title: Check Source Inconsistency
checkSourceInconsistency.description: Flag when different sources share the same translation

checkTargetInconsistency.title: Check Target Inconsistency
checkTargetInconsistency.description: Flag when the same source has different translations

displayOption.title: Inline Code Display
displayOption.description: Controls how inline codes are represented: original codes or generic markers or plain text

doc.overview:
The **Inconsistency Check** tool tracks the mapping between source text and its
translation across blocks and flags inconsistencies. By default it reports
target inconsistency: the same source string translated more than one way.
Optionally it can also report source inconsistency: distinct source strings that
share a single translation. Each block records a status (consistent or
inconsistent), the inconsistency type, and the alternative translations or
sources observed.

Source and target text are trimmed before comparison, and comparison can be
made case-insensitive. By default the tool compares segments across all input
documents; per-file mode resets its tracking state at the start of each document
so consistency is judged within a single file. A target locale is required.

caseSensitive.help:
When enabled, strings that differ only in letter case are treated as
distinct. Disable it to fold case differences together when judging
consistency.

checkPerFile.description: Judge consistency within each file rather than across all documents.
checkPerFile.help:
When enabled, the tool clears its source/target tracking at the start of
each document, so a string is only compared against others in the same file.
When disabled, comparison spans all input documents.

checkSourceInconsistency.description: Flag when different sources share the same translation.
checkSourceInconsistency.help:
When enabled, a block is flagged when its translation is also used for a
different source string. This catches cases where distinct meanings collapse
to one translation.

checkTargetInconsistency.description: Flag when the same source has different translations.
checkTargetInconsistency.help:
When enabled, a block is flagged when its source text has been translated
differently elsewhere. This is the primary inconsistency check.

displayOption.description: How inline codes are represented in the report.
displayOption.help:
Controls the rendering of inline codes when reporting alternatives:
`original` keeps the original code markup, `generic` uses numbered markers,
and `plain` strips codes to plain text.

limitations: Compares exact (trimmed) text; near-duplicate sources or translations are not detected.
Requires a configured target locale; blocks without a translation for it are skipped.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
Tracking state is global by default and reset per document only when per-file checking is enabled.

examples.title: Check both directions of inconsistency
examples.description: Flag both differing translations of one source and shared translations of different sources.

examples.title: Per-file consistency
examples.description: Judge consistency within each document independently.

entries.description: Checks for source entries that are the same but have different translations or target entries that are the same but have different sources. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Inconsistency Check
schema.description: Checks for source entries that are the same but have different translations or target entries that are the same but have different sources. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

checkInconststencies.title: Check Inconsistencies
checkInconststencies.description: Enable or disable inconsistency checking; allows the step to be toggled off without removing it from the pipeline.

checkPerFile.title: Per-File Checking
checkPerFile.description: Process each input file individually instead of comparing segments across all documents, reducing memory usage.

outputPath.title: Report File Path
outputPath.description: Full path of the XML report file to generate; supports ${rootDir} and ${inputRootDir} variables.

displayOption.title: Inline Code Display
displayOption.description: Controls how inline codes are represented in the report: original codes, generic markers, or plain text.

autoOpen.title: Auto-Open Report
autoOpen.description: Automatically open the generated report file in the default application after processing completes.

toolMeta.description: Checks for source entries that are the same but have different translations or target entries that are the same but have different sources. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: Generates a report of potential inconsistencies found by comparing source and target segments across multiple input documents. It checks for cases where the same source text has different translations, and where the same target text corresponds to different sources. The XML report groups findings by "key text" for easy review. Comparisons are case-sensitive, whitespace-sensitive, and ignore inline codes.

autoOpen.description: Automatically open the generated report file in the default application after processing completes.
autoOpen.help:
Automatically opens the generated XML report file in the system's default application after processing completes.

This is useful for interactive/desktop usage but should typically be disabled in automated pipelines or server environments.

checkInconststencies.description: Enable or disable inconsistency checking; allows the step to be toggled off without removing it from the pipeline.
checkInconststencies.help: Master toggle for the inconsistency checking functionality. Disabling this effectively turns the step into a pass-through, allowing you to disable it in a pipeline without removing it.
notes: This is a convenience toggle to disable the step within a pipeline configuration without removing it entirely.

checkPerFile.description: Process each input file individually instead of comparing segments across all documents, reducing memory usage.
checkPerFile.help:
Processes each input file individually rather than comparing segments across all files.

By default, segments are compared across **all** input documents, which requires keeping all segments in memory. Enabling this option limits comparison scope to a single file at a time, significantly reducing memory usage at the cost of not detecting cross-file inconsistencies.
notes: Consider enabling this option if you experience memory issues with large document sets.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this setting to have any effect

displayOption.description: Controls how inline codes are represented in the report: original codes, generic markers, or plain text.
displayOption.help:
Controls how inline codes are rendered in the generated report.

- **Original codes** (`original`): Inline codes appear as they do in the source documents
- **Generic markers** (`generic`): Codes are replaced with numbered markers like `<1>``</1>` or `<2/>`
- **Plain text** (`plainText`): All inline codes are stripped from the report output
displayOption.values: One of: `original` (original codes), `generic` (generic markers like `<1></1>`), `plainText` (inline codes removed)

outputPath.description: Full path of the XML report file to generate; supports ${rootDir} and ${inputRootDir} variables.
outputPath.help:
Full path for the generated XML inconsistency report.

Supported variables:
- `${rootDir}` — the pipeline root directory
- `${inputRootDir}` — the input root directory
outputPath.values: File path string. Supports `${rootDir}` and `${inputRootDir}` variables.
examples: `${rootDir}/inconsistency-report.xml` — default, writes to the pipeline root
`${inputRootDir}/qa/inconsistencies.xml` — writes to a qa subfolder under the input root

limitations: May run out of memory when processing a large number of segments across many files. Use the per-file option to mitigate.
Inline codes are ignored during comparisons — differences only in inline codes will not be flagged.
Comparisons are case-sensitive and whitespace-sensitive — `Hello` and `hello` are treated as different texts.
Text units or segments with no target are excluded from the check.
Empty text units or segments are included in the check.
processingNotes: The step collects all segments in memory (unless per-file mode is enabled) before generating the report, so memory usage scales with the total number of segments.
The step is a pass-through for filter events — it reads and analyzes segments but does not modify them.
The report is XML format, grouping inconsistencies by their "key text" with all corresponding source or target variants listed.

examples.title: Basic inconsistency check with default settings
examples.description: Runs inconsistency checking across all input documents and writes the report to the pipeline root directory.
examples.config:
```yaml
checkInconststencies: true
checkPerFile: false
outputPath: ${rootDir}/inconsistency-report.xml
displayOption: generic
autoOpen: true
```

examples.title: Large-scale batch check with reduced memory
examples.description: For processing a large number of files where cross-file memory usage is a concern, enable per-file checking and disable auto-open for automated pipelines.
examples.config:
```yaml
checkInconststencies: true
checkPerFile: true
outputPath: ${rootDir}/qa/inconsistency-report.xml
displayOption: generic
autoOpen: false
```

entries.description: Compare source and target for inline code differences. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Inline Codes Checker
schema.description: Compare source and target for inline code differences. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

codeDifference.title: Warn if there is a code difference between source and target segments
codeDifference.description: Verify target has the same inline codes as source.

guessOpenClose.title: Try to guess opening/closing types for placeholder codes
guessOpenClose.description: Guess which inline codes are opening and closing pairs.

extraCodesAllowed.title: Codes allowed to be extra in the target
extraCodesAllowed.description: Number of extra inline codes allowed in target beyond source.

missingCodesAllowed.title: Codes allowed to be missing from the target
missingCodesAllowed.description: Number of source inline codes allowed to be missing from target.

typesToIgnore.title: List of the inline code types to ignore
typesToIgnore.description: Semicolon-separated list of inline code types to exclude from checking.

toolMeta.description: Compare source and target for inline code differences. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: The Inline Codes Checker step compares the inline codes of each source and target and annotates differences. It reports codes present in the source but missing from the target, and codes present in the target but not in the source. It expects filter events and passes them through unchanged, attaching the detected issues for a downstream consumer to report. It is one of the granular quality checks that the aggregate Quality Check step combines into a single pass.
processingNotes: Operates on filter events; events are forwarded unchanged and the detected issues are accumulated per document.
Delegates to the verification library's `InlineCodesChecker`; its step parameters are a subset of the full Quality Check / CheckMate configuration.
Code difference checking (`codeDifference`) and opening/closing inference (`guessOpenClose`) are enabled by default.
Codes of the types listed in `typesToIgnore` are excluded from comparison; the default is `mrk;x-df-s;`.
`extraCodesAllowed` and `missingCodesAllowed` whitelist specific codes so that their presence or absence is not flagged.
Requires source and target locales; these are supplied by the pipeline rather than configured on the step.

examples.title: Verify inline codes are preserved between source and target
examples.description: Reports segments whose target adds or drops inline codes relative to the source, using open/close inference to pair tags. Annotation markers and the `x-df-s` type are ignored so they do not raise false positives.

entries.description: Removes inline codes from the text units content of a document. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Inline Codes Removal
schema.description: Removes inline codes from the text units content of a document. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

stripSource.title: Strip codes in the source text
stripSource.description: Apply the selected removal action to the source text of each text unit

stripTarget.title: Strip codes in the target text
stripTarget.description: Apply the selected removal action to the target text of each text unit

mode.title: What to remove
mode.description: Select what parts of the inline codes to remove

options.label: Remove code marker, but keep code content  ("<ph x='1'>[X]</ph>" ==> "[X]")
Remove code content, but keep code marker  ("<ph x='1'>[X]</ph>" ==> "<ph x='1'/>")
Remove code marker and code content  ("<ph x='1'>[X]</ph>" ==> "")

includeNonTranslatable.title: Apply to non-translatable text units
includeNonTranslatable.description: Apply the removal action even to text units marked as non-translatable

replaceWithSpace.title: Replace line break codes with spaces (When removing content AND marker)
replaceWithSpace.description: Replace line-break inline codes with spaces instead of removing them entirely (only when removing both marker and content)

toolMeta.description: Removes inline codes from the text units content of a document. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: Removes inline codes or content of inline codes from text units in a document. Inline codes are spans of data that should normally be left untouched inside translatable text (e.g., `<b>` tags in HTML). The step offers three removal modes: removing just the markers, just the content, or both. It operates on filter events and can selectively target source text, target text, and non-translatable units.

includeNonTranslatable.description: Apply the removal action even to text units marked as non-translatable
includeNonTranslatable.help: Controls whether the removal action is also applied to text units marked as **non-translatable**. When disabled, all non-translatable text units are left completely untouched.

mode.help:
An inline code has two parts: **markers** (always present) and **content** (optional).

For example, in XLIFF: `<bpt id="1"><b></bpt>` — the `<bpt>` / `</bpt>` tags are the **marker**, and `<b>` is the **content**.

- **Remove code marker, but keep code content** — The content becomes part of the regular text (e.g., the literal `<b>` appears as text).
- **Remove code content, but keep code marker** — The inline code still exists structurally but is generalized; original format data cannot be recovered.
- **Remove code marker and code content** — The entire inline code is removed with no trace left.
mode.values:
Integer values mapping to removal modes:
- `0` — Remove marker only, keep content
- `1` — Remove content only, keep marker
- `2` — Remove both marker and content

replaceWithSpace.description: Replace line-break inline codes with spaces instead of removing them entirely (only when removing both marker and content)
replaceWithSpace.help:
Replaces line-break inline codes with a space character instead of simply removing them. A code is treated as a line break if its type is `Code.TYPE_LB` or its content contains any of the following (case-insensitive): - `
`, `
`, `
` - `\n`, `\r` - `\u0085`, `\u2028`, `\u2029` This prevents words on adjacent lines from being concatenated when codes are stripped.
notes: This option only applies when the **Remove code marker and code content** mode is selected (mode = 2).

dependsOn.condition: only applies when mode is set to remove both marker and content (mode = 2)

stripSource.description: Apply the selected removal action to the source text of each text unit
stripSource.help: Applies the selected removal action to the **source** text of each text unit. When disabled, source inline codes are left intact regardless of the removal mode chosen.

stripTarget.description: Apply the selected removal action to the target text of each text unit
stripTarget.help: Applies the selected removal action to the **target** text of each text unit. When disabled, target inline codes are left intact regardless of the removal mode chosen.

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: The step operates on filter events — it must be placed after a filter in the pipeline.
Both source and target stripping are enabled by default, so the step processes both sides unless explicitly disabled.

examples.title: Strip all inline codes from XLIFF source and target
examples.description: Removes both markers and content from all text units, including non-translatable ones. This is the default configuration.
examples.config:
```yaml
stripSource: true
stripTarget: true
mode: 2
includeNonTranslatable: true
replaceWithSpace: false
```

examples.title: Generalize codes by removing content only
examples.description: Keeps the inline code structure but strips the original format data. Useful when you want to preserve code positioning but don't need the underlying markup.

examples.title: Replace line-break codes with spaces
examples.description:
When removing all code parts, line-break codes (e.g., `
` tags) are replaced with spaces to prevent words from merging.
examples.config:
```yaml
mode: 2
replaceWithSpace: true
stripSource: true
stripTarget: true
```

entries.description: Strip inline-code runs (placeholders and paired codes) from segments, leaving plain text.

includeNonTranslatable.title: Include Non-Translatable
includeNonTranslatable.description: Apply the removal action even to text units marked as non-translatable

replaceWithSpace.title: Replace With Space
replaceWithSpace.description: Replace line-break inline codes with spaces instead of removing them entirely

doc.overview:
The **Inline Codes Remove** tool removes inline-code runs — placeholders and
paired open/close codes — from segment content, leaving the surrounding plain
text. It is useful when a downstream consumer wants the textual content
without markup, for example to build a plain-text reference or to feed a tool
that cannot handle coded text.

Removal recurses into structured plural and select constructs so inline codes
inside them are also stripped. By default it applies to the target (a target
locale is required) and includes non-translatable blocks. Line-break codes
can optionally be replaced with a space so adjacent words do not run together.

applySource.description: Strip inline codes from source segments.
applySource.help: When enabled, inline-code runs are removed from the source segments.

applyTarget.description: Strip inline codes from target segments.
applyTarget.help:
When enabled, inline-code runs are removed from the target segments of the
configured locale.

dependsOn.condition: required when applyTarget is enabled

includeNonTranslatable.description: Also strip codes from non-translatable blocks.
includeNonTranslatable.help:
When enabled, the removal also applies to blocks marked non-translatable.
Disable it to leave non-translatable blocks untouched.

replaceWithSpace.description: Replace line-break codes with a space instead of removing them.
replaceWithSpace.help:
When enabled, line-break-style placeholder codes are replaced with a single space so that, for example, `foo
bar` becomes `foo bar` rather than `foobar`. Other inline codes are still removed entirely.

targetLocale.help: The locale whose target segments are processed. Required when `applyTarget` is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this to take effect

limitations: At least one of applySource or applyTarget must be enabled.
processingNotes: Removal recurses into plural and select constructs so codes inside them are stripped as well.
Paired open/close codes are dropped while their inner text content is retained.

examples.title: Strip codes from the target
examples.description: Produce plain target text for a locale.

examples.title: Keep word boundaries at line breaks
examples.description: Replace line-break codes with a space when stripping.
examples.config:
applyTarget: true
targetLocale: fr-FR
replaceWithSpace: true

entries.description: Merges adjacent inline codes in the source part of a text unit. Also where possible, moves leading and trailing codes of the source to the skeleton (Only for GenericSkeleton!). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Inline Codes Simplifier
schema.description: Merges adjacent inline codes in the source part of a text unit. Also where possible, moves leading and trailing codes of the source to the skeleton (Only for GenericSkeleton!). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

removeLeadingTrailingCodes.title: Remove leading and trailing codes
removeLeadingTrailingCodes.description: Removes leading and trailing codes from the source and place them in the skeleton.

mergeCodes.title: Merge codes
mergeCodes.description: Merges adjacent codes (leading/trailing codes are removed first (if option is enabled)).

rules.title: Simplifier Rules
rules.description: Code simplifier rules in Okapi Code Simplifier Rule Format for inline code normalization.

toolMeta.description: Merges adjacent inline codes in the source part of a text unit. Also where possible, moves leading and trailing codes of the source to the skeleton (Only for GenericSkeleton!). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: This step joins adjacent inline codes in text units, and optionally moves leading and trailing codes from the text unit to the skeleton. Only the source codes are affected, so it should be used **before** any target is created in the text unit. Takes filter events and sends filter events.

mergeCodes.help: Merges adjacent inline codes in text units into a single code. This simplifies segments where multiple codes appear next to each other with no translatable text between them.
notes: Only affects source codes — apply this step **before** any target is created in the text unit.

removeLeadingTrailingCodes.help:
Removes leading and trailing inline codes from text units and places them into the skeleton. This reduces the number of codes translators need to deal with, making segments cleaner.

Only works with `GenericSkeleton`-based filters.
notes: Only affects source codes — apply this step **before** any target is created in the text unit.

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: Only source codes are affected — this step should be placed in the pipeline **before** any step that creates target content in text units.
Leading/trailing code removal only works with `GenericSkeleton`-based content.

examples.title: Merge adjacent codes, keep boundary codes in place
examples.description: Collapse runs of adjacent inline codes into a single code while leaving leading and trailing codes inside the text unit. Useful when the goal is only to reduce code clutter mid-segment without altering the skeleton.

examples.title: Move boundary codes to the skeleton
examples.description: Apply the default behaviour: merge adjacent codes and push leading/trailing codes out of the text unit into the skeleton. Place this step before any target is created, since only source codes are affected and the skeleton must be `GenericSkeleton`-based.

entries.description: Apply format-specific tool chains to embedded child layers within a document.

doc.overview:
The **Layer Processor** intercepts embedded child layers — content of one
format nested inside another, such as HTML inside a JSON value — and runs a
format-specific tool chain over the parts of each child layer before emitting
them. It maps a child layer's format name to the chain of tools that should
process that layer's content.

When a child layer's format has no configured chain, its parts pass through
unchanged; non-child-layer parts always pass through. This tool is configured
programmatically: the pipeline-per-format mapping is supplied in Go code, so
it exposes no declarative parameters in flow or CLI configuration.
limitations: Configured programmatically only; the format-to-pipeline mapping is not expressible via flow or CLI config.
Only embedded child layers are intercepted; top-level layers and their parts pass through.
processingNotes: Parts of a child layer are buffered until the layer ends, then processed through the matching tool chain and re-emitted in order.
Child layers whose format has no configured pipeline pass through unchanged.

entries.description: Validate target length against absolute limits and source/target ratio thresholds.

checkMaxCharLength.title: Check Maximum Length Ratio
checkMaxCharLength.description: Warn if target exceeds a percentage of source character length

checkMinCharLength.title: Check Minimum Length Ratio
checkMinCharLength.description: Warn if target is shorter than a percentage of source character length

maxCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for Long Text (Max)
maxCharLengthAbove.description: Max percentage of source length allowed for long text

maxCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for Short Text (Max)
maxCharLengthBelow.description: Max percentage of source length allowed for short text

maxCharLengthBreak.title: Short/Long Threshold (Max)
maxCharLengthBreak.description: Character count threshold between short and long text for max check

maxChars.title: Maximum Characters
maxChars.description: Absolute maximum character count for target text (0 = disabled)

maxPercentage.title: Maximum Length Percentage
maxPercentage.description: Maximum target/source length ratio as percentage (0 = disabled)

maxWords.title: Maximum Words
maxWords.description: Maximum word count for target text (0 = disabled)

minCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for Long Text (Min)
minCharLengthAbove.description: Min percentage of source length allowed for long text

minCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for Short Text (Min)
minCharLengthBelow.description: Min percentage of source length allowed for short text

minCharLengthBreak.title: Short/Long Threshold (Min)
minCharLengthBreak.description: Character count threshold between short and long text for min check

minPercentage.title: Minimum Length Percentage
minPercentage.description: Minimum target/source length ratio as percentage (0 = disabled)

doc.overview:
The **Length Check** tool verifies that each translation stays within
configured length constraints and records any findings on the block's
properties as QA issues. It supports three kinds of limit: absolute caps on
character or word count; a simple maximum/minimum percentage of source length;
and a long/short ratio model that applies different percentage thresholds
depending on whether the source text is above or below a character breakpoint.
A target locale is required; blocks without a translation are skipped.

Each limit can be enabled independently, and any limit set to `0` is disabled.
The long/short ratio model mirrors the length-checker defaults used by the
Okapi Framework bridge, so its thresholds line up with the equivalent bridge
step.

checkMaxCharLength.description: Enable the maximum length-ratio check (long/short model).
checkMaxCharLength.help:
When enabled, the target is flagged if it exceeds a percentage of the source
length, where the percentage depends on whether the source is long or short.

checkMinCharLength.description: Enable the minimum length-ratio check (long/short model).
checkMinCharLength.help:
When enabled, the target is flagged if it falls below a percentage of the
source length, where the percentage depends on whether the source is long or
short.

maxCharLengthAbove.description: Maximum percentage of source length allowed for long text.
maxCharLengthAbove.help:
The maximum target/source ratio applied when the source is long (longer than
the maximum breakpoint).

maxCharLengthBelow.description: Maximum percentage of source length allowed for short text.
maxCharLengthBelow.help:
The maximum target/source ratio applied when the source is short (at or below
the maximum breakpoint). Short strings allow a higher ratio because small
length differences are proportionally large.

maxCharLengthBreak.description: Character threshold dividing short from long text (max check).
maxCharLengthBreak.help:
Source text longer than this many characters is treated as long for the
maximum-ratio check; shorter text is treated as short.

maxChars.description: Absolute maximum character count for the target.
maxChars.help:
Flags the target as an error when it exceeds this many characters. Set to
`0` to disable the absolute character cap.

maxPercentage.description: Maximum target/source length ratio, as a percentage.
maxPercentage.help:
Flags the target when its character length exceeds this percentage of the
source length. Set to `0` to disable.

maxWords.description: Absolute maximum word count for the target.
maxWords.help:
Flags the target as an error when it exceeds this many words. Set to `0` to
disable the absolute word cap.

minCharLengthAbove.description: Minimum percentage of source length required for long text.
minCharLengthAbove.help:
The minimum target/source ratio applied when the source is long (longer than
the minimum breakpoint).

minCharLengthBelow.description: Minimum percentage of source length required for short text.
minCharLengthBelow.help:
The minimum target/source ratio applied when the source is short (at or below
the minimum breakpoint).

minCharLengthBreak.description: Character threshold dividing short from long text (min check).
minCharLengthBreak.help:
Source text longer than this many characters is treated as long for the
minimum-ratio check; shorter text is treated as short.

minPercentage.description: Minimum target/source length ratio, as a percentage.
minPercentage.help:
Flags the target when its character length falls below this percentage of
the source length. Set to `0` to disable.

limitations: Percentage and ratio checks compare character lengths only when the source is non-empty.
Requires a configured target locale; blocks without a translation for it are skipped.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
Findings are stored on the block as a JSON array of QA issues.

examples.title: Cap absolute character length
examples.description: Flag any translation longer than 80 characters, useful for fixed-width UI strings.

examples.title: Constrain target/source ratio
examples.description: Require the target to be between 30% and 200% of the source length.

entries.description: Compare source and target lengths and annotate those that seem mismatched. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Length Checker
schema.description: Compare source and target lengths and annotate those that seem mismatched. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

checkStorageSize.title: Warn if a source or target text unit does not fit its ITS Storage Size property
checkStorageSize.description: Verify byte storage size of target text against ITS limits.

checkMaxCharLength.title: Warn if a target is longer than the given percentage of the character length of its source
checkMaxCharLength.description: Flag target text longer than a given percentage of source character length.

maxCharLengthBreak.title: Character length above which a text is considered "long"
maxCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered long for max length checking.

maxCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for "long" text
maxCharLengthAbove.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for long text.

maxCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for "short" text
maxCharLengthBelow.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for short text.

checkMinCharLength.title: Warn if a target is shorter than the given percentage of the character length of its source
checkMinCharLength.description: Flag target text shorter than a given percentage of source character length.

minCharLengthBreak.title: Character length above which a text is considered "long"
minCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered long for min length checking.

minCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for "long" text
minCharLengthAbove.description: Minimum allowed percentage of source length for long text.

minCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for "short" text
minCharLengthBelow.description: Minimum allowed percentage of source length for short text.

checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.title: Warn if a target is longer than
checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.description: Flag target text exceeding an absolute maximum character count.

absoluteMaxCharLength.title: Absolute Maximum Character Length
absoluteMaxCharLength.description: Maximum number of characters allowed in target text, regardless of source length.

toolMeta.description: Compare source and target lengths and annotate those that seem mismatched. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: The Length Checker step compares the length of the source and target of each text unit and annotates those whose ratio appears mismatched. It supports a maximum-length check and a minimum-length check, each expressed as a percentage of source length with separate thresholds for short and long text, an absolute maximum character count, and a storage-size check against limits declared by the document format. It expects filter events and passes them through unchanged, attaching the detected issues for a downstream consumer to report. It is one of the granular quality checks that the aggregate Quality Check step combines into a single pass.
limitations: Length is measured in characters, so the percentage thresholds must be tuned per language pair to avoid false positives.
The storage-size check only applies to formats that declare a size limit (for example the XLIFF `maxwidth` attribute).
processingNotes: Operates on filter events; events are forwarded unchanged and the detected issues are accumulated per document.
Delegates to the verification library's `LengthChecker`; its step parameters are a subset of the full Quality Check / CheckMate configuration.
By default the maximum-length check, minimum-length check, and storage-size check are enabled, while the absolute maximum-length check is disabled.

The percentage thresholds switch at a source-length break of 20 characters: for the maximum-length check, a source of 20 characters or fewer allows the target up to 350% of its length, while a longer source allows up to 200%; for the minimum-length check, a short source requires the target to be at least 30% of its length and a longer source at least 45%. The absolute maximum-length check defaults to 255 characters.
Requires source and target locales; these are supplied by the pipeline rather than configured on the step.

examples.title: Flag over-long translations with a hard character cap
examples.description: Checks target length against source length: for sources up to 20 characters the target may reach 350% of the source, and for longer sources up to 200%; any target over 255 characters outright is also flagged.
examples.config:
```yaml
checkMaxCharLength: true
maxCharLengthBreak: 20
maxCharLengthBelow: 350
maxCharLengthAbove: 200
checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength: true
absoluteMaxCharLength: 255
```

entries.description: Leverage existing translation into the text units content of a document. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Leveraging
schema.description: Leverage existing translation into the text units content of a document. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

leverage.title: Leverage Text Units
leverage.description: Enable leveraging of text units with existing translations.

noQueryThreshold.title: No-Query Threshold
noQueryThreshold.description: Skip query if an existing candidate scores at or above this value (101 = always query).

threshold.title: Minimum Match Score
threshold.description: Accept only matches with a score equal to or above this threshold.

fillTarget.title: Fill Target with Translation
fillTarget.description: Copy the best translation candidate into the target content.

fillTargetThreshold.title: Fill Target Threshold
fillTargetThreshold.description: Minimum match score required to copy the best candidate into the target.

fillIfTargetIsEmpty.title: Only If Target Is Empty
fillIfTargetIsEmpty.description: Fill the target only when it has no existing content.

fillIfTargetIsSameAsSource.title: Also If Target Equals Source
fillIfTargetIsSameAsSource.description: Also fill the target when its content is identical to the source.

downgradeIdenticalBestMatches.title: Downgrade Identical Exact Matches
downgradeIdenticalBestMatches.description: Reduce score by 1% when multiple identical exact matches are returned.

makeTMX.title: Generate TMX Document
makeTMX.description: Create a TMX file with all leveraged matches.

tmxPath.title: TMX Output Path
tmxPath.description: File path for the generated TMX document.

useMTPrefix.title: Add MT Prefix to Source
useMTPrefix.description: Add an MT-specific prefix to the source text for MT-origin entries.

useTargetPrefix.title: Add Prefix to Translation
useTargetPrefix.description: Add a prefix to leveraged translations based on match score.

targetPrefix.title: Target Prefix Text
targetPrefix.description: Prefix string to prepend to leveraged translations.

targetPrefixThreshold.title: Prefix Score Threshold
targetPrefixThreshold.description: Add the prefix when the match score is equal to or below this value.

copySourceOnNoText.title: Copy Source If No Text
copySourceOnNoText.description: Copy source into target when the source has no translatable text.

resourceClassName.title: Translation Resource
resourceClassName.description: Java class name of the translation resource connector (TM or MT engine).

resourceParameters.title: Resource Parameters
resourceParameters.description: Parameters for the translation resource connector (TM or MT engine).

toolMeta.description: Leverage existing translation into the text units content of a document. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: The Leveraging step sends extracted text units (or segments) to a translation resource — either a TM engine or an MT system — and retrieves matching translations. Matches above a configurable threshold are attached as annotations to the target, and optionally the best match can be copied directly into the target content. Non-translatable and approved text units are automatically skipped. Multiple Leveraging steps can be chained to query several resources in sequence (e.g., TM first, then MT for unmatched entries).

copySourceOnNoText.description: Copy source into target when the source has no translatable text.
copySourceOnNoText.help: Copies the source content into the target when the source contains no translatable text (only whitespace and/or inline codes). This copy overwrites any existing target content.

downgradeIdenticalBestMatches.description: Reduce score by 1% when multiple identical exact matches are returned.
downgradeIdenticalBestMatches.help: Reduces the score of identical exact matches by 1%. Some connectors return multiple exact matches that differ only in their translation. Because downstream components may trigger automated processes on exact matches, this prevents such triggers when the best match is ambiguous.
notes: Only affects cases where multiple exact matches are returned with identical source text but different translations.

fillIfTargetIsEmpty.description: Fill the target only when it has no existing content.
fillIfTargetIsEmpty.help: Restricts target filling to only text units where the target is currently empty. Prevents overwriting existing translations.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this to take effect

fillIfTargetIsSameAsSource.description: Also fill the target when its content is identical to the source.
fillIfTargetIsSameAsSource.help: Allows overwriting target content when it is identical (text and codes) to the source. Useful for catching untranslated entries that were initialized with source content.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this option to be enabled
must be true for this to take effect

fillTarget.description: Copy the best translation candidate into the target content.
fillTarget.help: Copies the best translation candidate directly into the target content of the text unit, provided its score meets the `fillTargetThreshold`. The match is still added as an annotation regardless of this setting.

dependsOn.condition: controls the minimum score for filling

fillTargetThreshold.description: Minimum match score required to copy the best candidate into the target.
fillTargetThreshold.help: Minimum match score for copying the best candidate into the target content. If the best match scores below this value, the translation is **not** copied into the target — but it is still attached as an annotation/candidate.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this to take effect

leverage.description: Enable leveraging of text units with existing translations.
leverage.help: Master switch for the step. Allows you to keep the step configured in your pipeline while toggling it on and off without removing it.

makeTMX.description: Create a TMX file with all leveraged matches.
makeTMX.help: Creates a single TMX document containing all entries for which a match was found across all input files.

dependsOn.condition: must specify the output file path

noQueryThreshold.description: Skip query if an existing candidate scores at or above this value (101 = always query).
noQueryThreshold.help:
If a text unit already has a translation candidate with a score equal to or above this value, no query is sent to the translation resource. This avoids unnecessary (and potentially costly) lookups when a good-enough match already exists.

Set to **101** to always query the resource regardless of existing candidates.
noQueryThreshold.values: Integer score from 0–101. Use `101` to always allow the query.
examples: `100` — skip query if an exact match already exists
`95` — skip query if a 95%+ match already exists

resourceClassName.description: Java class name of the translation resource connector (TM or MT engine).
resourceClassName.help:
Fully qualified Java class name of the translation resource connector to use. Can be a TM connector (e.g., Pensieve) or an MT connector. The list of available connectors depends on your installation. Internet-based resources may be slow and result in lengthy processing time.

See the Okapi **Connectors** documentation for available options.
resourceClassName.values: Fully qualified Java class name implementing `ITranslationResource`. Default is `net.sf.okapi.connectors.pensieve.PensieveTMConnector`.

targetPrefix.description: Prefix string to prepend to leveraged translations.
targetPrefix.help: The prefix string inserted at the front of leveraged translations when their match score is at or below `targetPrefixThreshold`.
examples: `FUZZY__` — default prefix for identifying fuzzy matches

dependsOn.condition: must be true

targetPrefixThreshold.description: Add the prefix when the match score is equal to or below this value.
targetPrefixThreshold.help: The prefix is added to the leveraged translation only when the best match score is **equal to or below** this value. Matches above this threshold are copied without a prefix.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

threshold.description: Accept only matches with a score equal to or above this threshold.
threshold.help: Minimum match score for a translation to be accepted and attached as an annotation to the text unit. Matches below this score are discarded entirely.

tmxPath.description: File path for the generated TMX document.
tmxPath.help: Full path for the generated TMX document. Supports pipeline variables for dynamic paths.
tmxPath.values: File path. Supports variables: `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and locale variables (`${srcLoc}`, `${trgLoc}`, etc.).

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this path to be used

useMTPrefix.description: Add an MT-specific prefix to the source text for MT-origin entries.
useMTPrefix.help: Adds an MT-specific prefix to the source text when the match originates from an MT engine. This ensures the entry is treated as a fuzzy match in tools that cannot identify MT origin via the `creationid` attribute.

useTargetPrefix.description: Add a prefix to leveraged translations based on match score.
useTargetPrefix.help: Adds a configurable prefix to the front of leveraged translations copied into the target, based on the match score. Useful for flagging fuzzy matches (e.g., prefixing with `FUZZY__`) so translators can identify them.

dependsOn.condition: must be true — prefix is only added when a match is copied into target
defines the prefix text
controls the score threshold for adding the prefix

processingNotes: Non-translatable text units are automatically skipped.
Text units with the `approved` property set to `yes` are automatically skipped.
Multiple Leveraging steps can be chained in a pipeline to query several translation resources in sequence (e.g., TM first, then MT for unmatched entries).
Matches are attached as annotations to the target container or target segments regardless of the fill-target settings.
Internet-based translation resources (e.g., cloud MT) may result in lengthy processing time.
A single TMX output document is generated across all input files when TMX generation is enabled.

examples.title: TM leveraging with fuzzy prefix
examples.description: Leverage from a Pensieve TM, fill target for 95%+ matches, and prefix fuzzy matches for translator review.
examples.config:
```yaml
leverage: true
resourceClassName: net.sf.okapi.connectors.pensieve.PensieveTMConnector
threshold: 70
fillTarget: true
fillTargetThreshold: 95
useTargetPrefix: true
targetPrefix: "FUZZY__"
targetPrefixThreshold: 99
```

examples.title: Chained TM + MT leveraging
examples.description: Use two Leveraging steps in sequence: the first queries a TM and the second uses MT only for entries without an existing good match. Set `noQueryThreshold` on the second step to skip entries already matched by the TM.
examples.config:
```yaml
# Step 1 — TM
leverage: true
resourceClassName: net.sf.okapi.connectors.pensieve.PensieveTMConnector
threshold: 70
fillTarget: true
fillTargetThreshold: 95
noQueryThreshold: 101

# Step 2 — MT (only for unmatched)
leverage: true
resourceClassName: net.sf.okapi.connectors.microsoft.MicrosoftMTConnector
threshold: 0
fillTarget: true
fillTargetThreshold: 0
noQueryThreshold: 95
useMTPrefix: true
```

entries.description: Normalize line endings in block text to LF, CRLF, or CR.

doc.overview:
The **Line Break Convert** tool normalizes line endings in block text to a
chosen style. It first collapses every line ending (`\r\n` and `\r`) to `\n`,
then rewrites them to the target style. Use it to make output consistent for
a platform that expects a particular convention — for example CRLF on
Windows-targeted files.

By default both the source and every target locale are converted.

applySource.help: When enabled, line endings in the source text are normalized.

applyTarget.help:
When enabled, line endings are normalized in the target text of every
target locale present on the block.

mode.help:
`lf` produces Unix line feeds (`\n`), `crlf` produces Windows carriage
return + line feed (`\r\n`), and `cr` produces classic carriage returns
(`\r`).

processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks only; non-translatable blocks pass through unchanged.
Target conversion applies to all target locales present, not a single configured locale.

examples.title: Convert to Windows line endings
examples.description: Rewrite all line endings to CRLF for source and targets.

examples.title: Normalize only the source
examples.description: Convert source line endings to LF, leaving targets untouched.

entries.description: Convert the type of line-breaks in a text-based file. Expects: raw document. Sends back: raw document.

schema.title: Line-Break Conversion
schema.description: Convert the type of line-breaks in a text-based file. Expects: raw document. Sends back: raw document.

lineBreak.title: Convert line-breaks to the following type
lineBreak.description: Select the new type of line-break for the output.

toolMeta.description: Convert the type of line-breaks in a text-based file. Expects: raw document. Sends back: raw document.

doc.overview: Converts all line-breaks in a text-based input document to a specified type (DOS/Windows, Unix/Linux, or Macintosh). The step auto-detects existing line-break types and handles mixed line-break styles within a single file. Input encoding is preserved in the output, and Byte-Order-Marks are kept if present.

lineBreak.help:
Controls which line-break style is written to the output file. The input file's line-break types are auto-detected, and mixed styles are supported — all are normalized to the chosen type.

| Value | Line-Break | Hex | Escape |
|---|---|---|---|
| `$0d$$0a$` | DOS/Windows (CR+LF) | `0x0D 0x0A` | `\r\n` |
| `$0a$` | Unix/Linux (LF) | `0x0A` | `\n` |
| `$0d$` | Macintosh (CR) | `0x0D` | `\r` |
lineBreak.values: Encoded line-break string using `$hex$` notation: `$0d$$0a$` for CR+LF, `$0a$` for LF, `$0d$` for CR.
notes: The input encoding must be specified explicitly, even for formats that usually auto-detect encoding (XML, HTML, etc.). The only exception is files with a Byte-Order-Mark.
The output encoding always matches the input encoding — no transcoding occurs. BOMs are preserved if present.

limitations: None known.
processingNotes: Input encoding must be specified explicitly for all formats except BOM-prefixed files.
Output encoding always matches input encoding — no transcoding is performed.
Byte-Order-Marks are preserved if present in the input, but never added or removed.
Mixed line-break types within a single input file are handled correctly — all are converted to the target type.

examples.title: Convert Windows line endings to Unix
examples.description: Normalize a Windows-formatted file to Unix line endings, commonly needed when moving files between platforms or preparing source files for version control.

entries.description: Compare source and target number, date and time patterns to assure they are localized. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Localizable Quality Check
schema.description: Compare source and target number, date and time patterns to assure they are localized. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

toolMeta.description: Compare source and target number, date and time patterns to assure they are localized. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: This step checks that all localizables (dates, times, and numbers) exist in the target and are localized correctly for the target locale. It compares source and target number, date, and time patterns, adding issue annotations if a target localizable is missing or not properly localized. Both source and target languages must be set for the step to function.
limitations: Numbers that use different units in the source and target will be flagged as false positives (e.g., imperial vs. metric measurements, currency conversions).
processingNotes: Requires both source and target language to be set in the pipeline for locale-aware checking.
Produces issue annotations on segments where target localizables are missing or incorrectly localized — does not modify content.
Checks dates, times (in various formats), and numbers.

entries.description: Annotates text units with Microsoft Translator matches or/and creates a TM from them. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Microsoft Batch Translation
schema.description: Annotates text units with Microsoft Translator matches or/and creates a TM from them. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

category.title: Category
category.description: Category code if accessing a trained system

configPath.title: Engine mapping
configPath.description: Full path of the properties file listing the engines

annotate.title: Annotate the text units with the translations
annotate.description: Adds translation match annotations to text units for use by subsequent steps

makeTmx.title: Generate a TMX document
makeTmx.description: Creates a TMX translation memory document from the translation results

tmxPath.description: Full path of the new TMX document to create
tmxPath.title: TMX Output Path

markAsMT.title: Mark the generated translation as machine translation results
markAsMT.description: Marks generated TM entries with a machine translation origin indicator (e.g., creationId="MT!")

maxEvents.title: Events buffer
maxEvents.description: Number of events to store before sending a query

threshold.title: Threshold
threshold.description: Score below which matches are not retained

fillTarget.title: Fill the target with the best translation candidate
fillTarget.description: Copies the best-scoring translation candidate into empty target segments

fillTargetThreshold.title: Fill threshold
fillTargetThreshold.description: Fill the target when the best candidate is equal or above this score

onlyWhenWithoutCandidate.title: Query only entries without existing candidate
onlyWhenWithoutCandidate.description: Sends only text units without existing translation candidates to the MT engine

sendTmx.title: Send the TMX document to the next step
sendTmx.description: Passes the generated TMX document as input to the next pipeline step instead of filter events

useTargetPrefix.title: Add a prefix to the translation candidate copied in the target
useTargetPrefix.description: Prepends a prefix string to translation candidates copied into the target

targetPrefix.title: Target prefix
targetPrefix.description: Text to use as the target prefix

toolMeta.description: Annotates text units with Microsoft Translator matches or/and creates a TM from them. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: This step annotates text units with [Microsoft Translator](http://www.microsofttranslator.com) candidates and/or creates a TMX translation memory from them. It processes multiple text units in batches for significantly faster throughput compared to using the Leveraging Step with the Microsoft Translator Connector individually. Text units flagged as non-translatable are skipped.

annotate.description: Adds translation match annotations to text units for use by subsequent steps
annotate.help: Adds translation match annotations to text units so subsequent pipeline steps can use them. Existing annotations from prior steps are preserved.

azureKey.help:
The Azure subscription key used to authenticate with the Microsoft Translator v3 API. If you previously had a v2 key, the same key should work with v3.

Obtain a key from the [Azure Cognitive Services pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/cognitive-services/).
notes: The v2 API was retired on 2019-04-30. You must use the v3 API with an Azure key.
You must respect Microsoft's Terms of Service. Commercial or high-volume usage requires a commercial license agreement.

category.help:
An optional identifier for accessing a trained custom engine from Microsoft Translator Hub.

You can specify either:
- A direct engine identifier (the 'category' from Microsoft Translator Hub)
- A keyword in the form `@@@keyword@@@` — requires a properties file in **Engine mapping**

The keyword can be a literal string or the `${domain}` variable. When `${domain}` is used, it is replaced by the first ITS Domain annotation found on a text unit. All batches translated before a domain annotation is found use the empty (generic) category.
category.values: Direct engine ID string, or `@@@keyword@@@` pattern. The keyword portion is case-sensitive, without spaces, equals signs, or periods.
notes: Only the **first** occurrence of the ITS Domain annotation has an effect on engine selection.

Because this step works in batches, segments before the first Domain annotation but within the same batch will still be translated with the engine specified by the domain. For example, if the buffer is 50 events and the domain annotation is on event 60, events 1–50 use the generic engine while events 51–100 (including 51–59) use the domain engine.
examples: `@@@travel@@@` — uses the engine mapped to the `travel` keyword
`${domain}` — dynamically selects engine based on ITS Domain annotation

configPath.help:
Path to a properties file that maps category keywords to Microsoft Translator Hub engine identifiers. Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and locale variables (`${srcLoc}`, `${trgLoc}`, etc.).

The file format is:
```
<keyword>.<language>=<engineID>
```
Where `<keyword>` is case-sensitive (no spaces, `=`, or `.`) and `<language>` is the uppercase target language code.
configPath.values: File path to a `.properties` file. Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, `${srcLoc}`, `${trgLoc}` variables.
examples: A fallback mechanism applies: `@@@client2.law@@@` first looks for `client2.law.DE`, then falls back to `client2.DE`. If no custom engine is found, the generic Microsoft engine is used.

fillTarget.description: Copies the best-scoring translation candidate into empty target segments
fillTarget.help: Copies the highest-scoring translation candidate into the target segment (if the target is currently empty and the score meets or exceeds the **Fill threshold**). Only matches returned by Microsoft Translator are considered — not candidates from other sources.

dependsOn.condition: controls the minimum score required for filling

fillTargetThreshold.help: The minimum score a translation candidate must have before it is copied into the target. If the best match scores below this value, the target is left empty.

makeTmx.description: Creates a TMX translation memory document from the translation results
makeTmx.help: Creates a TMX translation memory file from the Microsoft Translator results. The output path is specified in the **TMX path** field. If a file already exists at that path, it will be overwritten.

markAsMT.description: Marks generated TM entries with a machine translation origin indicator (e.g., creationId="MT!")
markAsMT.help: Marks generated TM entries as machine translation output. For example, the `creationId` attribute of the TMX `<Tu>` element is set to `"MT!"`.

maxEvents.help: Controls how many filter events are buffered before sending a single batch query to the MT engine. Larger buffers mean faster processing, but the Microsoft API has limits on the volume of text per request.
maxEvents.values: Positive integer. Larger values improve throughput but may hit API text volume limits.

maxMatches.help: Limits the number of translation candidates returned per source text segment.

onlyWhenWithoutCandidate.description: Sends only text units without existing translation candidates to the MT engine
onlyWhenWithoutCandidate.help: Only sends text units to Microsoft Translator when they have no existing translation candidates — i.e., no annotations added by previous pipeline steps or present in the original document. This avoids redundant API calls for already-leveraged segments.

sendTmx.description: Passes the generated TMX document as input to the next pipeline step instead of filter events
sendTmx.help: When enabled, the generated TMX document becomes the **only** input passed to the next pipeline step (as a raw document), replacing the filter events. If disabled, the original filter events continue through the pipeline.

targetPrefix.help: The text string prepended to translation candidates when they are copied into the target. Only applied when **Add a prefix** is enabled.
examples: `!MT__` — default prefix to flag machine-translated content

threshold.help: Translation matches scoring below this value are discarded. See the Microsoft Translator Connector documentation for how scores are computed from match degree and rating values.

tmxPath.help: The full file path where the generated TMX document will be written. Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and locale variables (`${srcLoc}`, `${trgLoc}`, etc.).
examples: `${rootDir}/tmFromMS.tmx` — writes to the root directory of the project

useTargetPrefix.description: Prepends a prefix string to translation candidates copied into the target
useTargetPrefix.help: Prepends a configurable prefix string to any translation candidate copied into the target. Useful for visually flagging MT output during review.

dependsOn.condition: specifies the prefix text to use
must be enabled for prefix to apply

limitations: The Microsoft Translator API has restrictions for high-volume usage — contact Microsoft for details.
Only the **first** ITS Domain annotation encountered in a batch affects engine selection.
The v3 API no longer supports the translation memory function that was available in v2.
The v3 implementation does not support newer features such as profanity filtering.
See also the limitations on the Microsoft Translator Connector.
processingNotes: Text units flagged as non-translatable are not sent for translation.
Using the Leveraging Step with the Microsoft Translator Connector produces similar results, but this batch step is **much faster** because it processes multiple text units per API call.
