MT output quality can sometimes be improved post-processing — e.g., extra or missing spaces around inline codes can be fixed with the Space Check Step.
Because batching is used, ITS Domain annotations may affect segments before and after the annotation within the same batch boundary.

examples.title: Engine mapping properties file
examples.description: Example properties file for mapping category keywords to Microsoft Translator Hub engine identifiers. The format is `<keyword>.<LANGUAGE>=<engineID>`. A fallback mechanism tries progressively shorter keyword prefixes.

examples.title: Category keyword with domain variable
examples.description: Using `${domain}` as the category keyword dynamically selects the engine based on the first ITS Domain annotation found in the text units. Set the Category field to `@@@${domain}@@@` and provide an engine mapping file.

entries.description: Translate block content using Microsoft Translator.

schema.title: Microsoft Translator Translate
schema.description: Translate content using Microsoft Translator

toolMeta.description: Translate content using Microsoft Translator

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: API key for the MT provider

email.title: Email
email.description: Account email for higher rate limits (mymemory)

doc.overview:
The **Microsoft Translator** tool translates untranslated block content with
the Microsoft Translator (Azure Cognitive Services) API. The provider is fixed
by the tool name, so there is no provider field. A target language is required
and an Azure subscription key must be supplied; the Azure region of the
Translator resource may also be required depending on how the resource is
provisioned. If the dedicated subscription key is empty, the shared `apiKey`
field is used as a fallback so keychain-based credential resolution works
uniformly.

Blocks that carry inline codes are round-tripped through semantic HTML so the
API preserves the tags natively, then reconstructed from the response. With a
session cache, targets already translated in a previous run are reused. When
constructed without credentials the tool falls back to an offline demo
provider so it remains listable and runnable for illustration.

apiKey.description: Fallback credential used as the subscription key when subscriptionKey is empty.
apiKey.help:
Part of the shared MT credential schema. For Microsoft Translator it is
used as the subscription key only when `subscriptionKey` is not set, so
keychain credential resolution (which injects `apiKey`) works uniformly.

email.help: Part of the shared MT credential schema; consumed only by the MyMemory tool.

projectId.help: Part of the shared MT credential schema; consumed only by the Google Translate tool.

region.description: Azure region of the Translator resource.
region.help:
The Azure region (for example `westeurope`) of the Translator resource.
Required when the resource is provisioned to a specific region rather than
the global endpoint.

subscriptionKey.description: Azure subscription key for the Translator resource.
subscriptionKey.help:
The Azure Cognitive Services subscription key. Required to call the real
API; without it (and without the `apiKey` fallback) the tool falls back to
the offline demo provider.

limitations: Requires a target language and an Azure subscription key; without a key the tool runs the offline demo provider, not Microsoft Translator.
The region must match the Translator resource when it is provisioned to a specific Azure region.
The provider makes a network round-trip per block; cost and latency scale with content volume.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks with non-empty source; other parts pass through unchanged.
Blocks with inline codes round-trip through semantic HTML so tags survive translation.
When subscriptionKey is empty the shared apiKey field is used as the subscription key.

doc.overview:
The **ModernMT Translate** tool translates untranslated block content with the
ModernMT API. The provider is fixed by the tool name, so there is no provider
field — only the ModernMT credential applies. A target language is required and
a ModernMT API key must be supplied (resolved from the OS keychain by the CLI,
or set inline in a recipe step).

Blocks that carry inline codes are round-tripped through semantic HTML so the
API preserves the tags natively, then reconstructed from the response. With a
session cache, targets already translated in a previous run are reused, so
repeat runs skip blocks that are already done. When constructed without
credentials the tool falls back to an offline demo provider so it remains
listable and runnable for illustration.

The provider is fixed to ModernMT by the tool name and cannot be changed through configuration.

entries.description: Indicates a translation coming from an MT engine. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: MT Character Count
schema.description: Indicates a translation coming from an MT engine. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

toolMeta.description: Indicates a translation coming from an MT engine. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: Counts the characters in text units whose top alternate translation is an `MT` match — a translation produced by a machine-translation engine rather than retrieved from a translation memory. It is the character-counting counterpart of the MT Word Count Step, differing only in that it tallies characters rather than words.
processingNotes: Operates per segment: it inspects the first (top-ranked) `AltTranslation` on each target segment's `AltTranslationsAnnotation` and counts a segment only when that match type is exactly `MT`.
Counting is performed on the **source** text, not the target — the character total reflects the source volume covered by machine translation.
Uses the GMX-V character counter; the only behavioural difference from the word-count variant is words versus characters.
Counts only translatable text units; empty text units and non-translatable ones are skipped.

Results are stored as annotations on filter events and aggregated to the configured levels (text unit, group, document, sub-document, batch item, batch); the step produces no output document of its own.

Belongs to the `OKAPI_CHARACTER_COUNTS` category group. The `CategoryResolver` keeps only the highest-ranked matching category per segment — `MT` ranks below all TM-derived categories (exact, fuzzy, phrase-assembled) and above `CONCORDANCE`.

entries.description: Indicates a translation coming from an MT engine. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: MT Word Count
schema.description: Indicates a translation coming from an MT engine. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

toolMeta.description: Indicates a translation coming from an MT engine. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: Counts the words in text units whose top alternate translation is an `MT` match — a translation produced by a machine-translation engine rather than retrieved from a translation memory. The count is recorded as a word-count metric in a `MetricsAnnotation` so that scoping and pricing steps can report machine-translated volume separately from TM-leveraged content, which is usually rated under different terms.
processingNotes: Operates per segment: it inspects the first (top-ranked) `AltTranslation` on each target segment's `AltTranslationsAnnotation` and counts a segment only when that match type is exactly `MT`.
Counting is performed on the **source** text, not the target — the word total reflects the source volume covered by machine translation.
Words are counted with the GMX-V word counter; the parallel character-count variant counts characters instead.
Counts only translatable text units; empty text units and non-translatable ones are skipped.

Results are stored as annotations on filter events and aggregated to the configured levels (text unit, group, document, sub-document, batch item, batch); the step produces no output document of its own.

Belongs to the `OKAPI_WORD_COUNTS` category group. The `CategoryResolver` keeps only the highest-ranked matching category per segment — `MT` ranks below all TM-derived categories (exact, fuzzy, phrase-assembled) and above `CONCORDANCE`.

entries.description: Align paragraphs (text units) between a source and a target document. Only TextUnit events are passed along - all other events are lost

schema.title: Paragraph Alignment
schema.description: Align paragraphs (text units) between a source and a target document. Only TextUnit events are passed along - all other events are lost

toolMeta.description: Align paragraphs (text units) between a source and a target document. Only TextUnit events are passed along - all other events are lost

doc.overview: Aligns paragraphs (TextUnits) from two documents — a source and a target. Internally uses `ResourceSimplifier` to flatten events and expand TextUnits so all are available for alignment. Only TextUnit events are passed along; all other event types are discarded. Sends a special `PIPELINE_PARAMETERS` event to inform subsequent steps (e.g., Sentence Aligner) that the target input has been consumed.

outputOneToOneMatchesOnly.help:
Controls whether only cleanly paired (1-to-1) paragraph alignments are output, or whether many-to-many alignments are also included.

When enabled, paragraphs that could not be matched to exactly one counterpart are dropped from the output. This produces a cleaner but potentially smaller set of aligned pairs.

useSkeletonAlignment.help:
Uses the document skeleton (structural markup surrounding text) as additional anchor points during alignment.

Standard Gale & Church paragraph alignment can drift on long runs of paragraphs. Skeleton alignment exploits formatting cues to provide better anchoring, which may improve results for markup-rich formats (HTML, XML, DITA, etc.).
notes: **Experimental** — results may vary depending on the document format and how much structural markup is present.

limitations: Standard Gale & Church alignment is not accurate for long runs of paragraphs without additional anchoring.
Only TextUnit events are passed through — all other event types (document parts, skeleton, sub-documents) are lost.
The target input is consumed by this step and is no longer available to subsequent steps.
processingNotes: `ResourceSimplifier` is called internally to flatten events and expand TextUnits before alignment.
A special `PIPELINE_PARAMETERS` event is sent downstream to inform subsequent steps (e.g., Sentence Aligner) that the target input has been consumed.

examples.title: Keep only clean 1-to-1 paragraph pairs
examples.description: Emit only paragraphs that aligned to exactly one counterpart, dropping many-to-many groupings. This is the default and produces the cleaner pairs typically wanted before sentence alignment.

examples.title: Use skeleton anchors for markup-rich input
examples.description: Enable the experimental skeleton alignment so structural markup (HTML/XML/DITA tags surrounding text) provides extra anchor points. This can reduce drift on long runs of paragraphs where length-based alignment alone is unreliable.

entries.description: Compare source and target for pattern differences. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Patterns Checker
schema.description: Compare source and target for pattern differences. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

checkPatterns.title: Verify that the following source patterns are translated as expected
checkPatterns.description: Enable verification of source/target pattern consistency using regex rules.

patterns.description: Regex pattern pairs for verifying source-target consistency.
patterns.title: Pattern Rules

toolMeta.description: Compare source and target for pattern differences. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: The Patterns Checker step verifies that content matching a configured regular-expression pattern in one side of a segment has its expected counterpart in the other side. Each pattern rule pairs a source regex with a target regex (or the `<same>` keyword, meaning the exact source match must reappear in the target), and a direction that decides which side is searched first. It expects filter events and passes them through unchanged, attaching the detected issues for a downstream consumer to report. It is one of the granular quality checks that the aggregate Quality Check step combines into a single pass.
processingNotes: Operates on filter events; events are forwarded unchanged and the detected issues are accumulated per document.
Delegates to the verification library's `PatternsChecker`; its step parameters are a subset of the full Quality Check / CheckMate configuration.

Pattern checking is enabled by default and ships with a built-in rule set: opening parentheses, closing parentheses, other bracketing characters, email addresses, URLs, IP addresses, C-style printf codes, and tripled letters.

Each rule carries a severity (low, medium, or high); the built-in URL, email, IP and printf rules expect `<same>`, while the tripled-letter rule is checked from the target side rather than the source.
Requires source and target locales; these are supplied by the pipeline rather than configured on the step.

examples.title: Require numbers in the source to appear in the target
examples.description: Adds a custom rule alongside the built-in pattern set: every run of digits found in the source (`fromSource: true`) must also occur in the target, otherwise the segment is flagged at warning severity.
examples.config:
```yaml
checkPatterns: true
patterns:
  - enabled: true
    fromSource: true
    source: "\\d+"
    target: "\\d+"
    severity: 1
    description: "Numbers must be preserved in the target"
```

entries.description: Verify that configured regex patterns are preserved or absent in target text.

doc.overview:
The **Pattern Check** tool validates target translations against a set of regex
rules and records findings on the block's properties as QA issues. Each rule
either requires a pattern to be preserved — the number of matches in the target
must equal the number in the source — or forbids a pattern from appearing in the
target at all. Preserved-pattern rules are typically used for placeholders,
variables, and format specifiers; forbidden-pattern rules catch markup or text
that must not survive into the translation.

A target locale is required. The set of patterns is supplied programmatically
rather than through the schema fields, so this tool is most often configured by
the host application or a flow definition that provides the rules.
limitations: The pattern rules themselves are configured programmatically; they are not exposed as schema parameters.
Requires a configured target locale; a block with no translation passes with no issues recorded.
A rule cannot both require and forbid the same pattern.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
Preserved-pattern rules compare match counts between source and target; forbidden-pattern rules check for any match in the target.

examples.title: Add to a flow
examples.description: The pattern rules are supplied programmatically; reference the tool with an empty configuration and provide rules from the host.

entries.description: Matches assembled from phrases in the TM or other resources (different algorithms could be used). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Phrase Assembled Character Count
schema.description: Matches assembled from phrases in the TM or other resources (different algorithms could be used). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

toolMeta.description: Matches assembled from phrases in the TM or other resources (different algorithms could be used). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: Counts the characters in text units whose top alternate translation is a `PHRASE_ASSEMBLED` match — a result assembled from individual phrases held in the translation memory or other resources, rather than retrieved as a single stored segment. It is the character-counting counterpart of the Phrase Assembled Word Count Step, differing only in that it tallies characters rather than words.
limitations: The Okapi source notes that, as of its writing, no leveraging step yet annotates segments with the `PHRASE_ASSEMBLED` match type, so in practice this counter typically reports zero unless an upstream component supplies such matches.
processingNotes: Operates per segment: it inspects the first (top-ranked) `AltTranslation` on each target segment's `AltTranslationsAnnotation` and counts a segment only when that match type is exactly `PHRASE_ASSEMBLED`.
Counting is performed on the **source** text, not the target — the character total reflects the source volume leveraged at this match level.
Uses the GMX-V character counter; the only behavioural difference from the word-count variant is words versus characters.
Counts only translatable text units; empty text units and non-translatable ones are skipped.

Results are stored as annotations on filter events and aggregated to the configured levels (text unit, group, document, sub-document, batch item, batch); the step produces no output document of its own.

Belongs to the `OKAPI_CHARACTER_COUNTS` category group. The `CategoryResolver` keeps only the highest-ranked matching category per segment — `PHRASE_ASSEMBLED` ranks below all fuzzy categories and above `MT` and `CONCORDANCE`.

entries.description: Matches assembled from phrases in the TM or other resources (different algorithms could be used). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Phrase Assembled Word Count
schema.description: Matches assembled from phrases in the TM or other resources (different algorithms could be used). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

toolMeta.description: Matches assembled from phrases in the TM or other resources (different algorithms could be used). Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: Counts the words in text units whose top alternate translation is a `PHRASE_ASSEMBLED` match — a result assembled from individual phrases held in the translation memory or other resources, rather than retrieved as a single stored segment. The count is recorded as a word-count metric in a `MetricsAnnotation` so that scoping and pricing steps can report assembled-from-fragments volume as its own category, distinct from whole-segment fuzzy or exact matches.
limitations: The Okapi source notes that, as of its writing, no leveraging step yet annotates segments with the `PHRASE_ASSEMBLED` match type, so in practice this counter typically reports zero unless an upstream component supplies such matches.
processingNotes: Operates per segment: it inspects the first (top-ranked) `AltTranslation` on each target segment's `AltTranslationsAnnotation` and counts a segment only when that match type is exactly `PHRASE_ASSEMBLED`.
Counting is performed on the **source** text, not the target — the word total reflects the source volume leveraged at this match level.
Words are counted with the GMX-V word counter; the parallel character-count variant counts characters instead.
Counts only translatable text units; empty text units and non-translatable ones are skipped.

Results are stored as annotations on filter events and aggregated to the configured levels (text unit, group, document, sub-document, batch item, batch); the step produces no output document of its own.

Belongs to the `OKAPI_WORD_COUNTS` category group. The `CategoryResolver` keeps only the highest-ranked matching category per segment — `PHRASE_ASSEMBLED` ranks below all fuzzy categories and above `MT` and `CONCORDANCE`.

entries.description: Verify interpolation placeholders and numbered tags are preserved in the target.

flagExtra.title: Flag extra placeholders
flagExtra.description: Report placeholders present in the target but absent from the source

doc.overview:
The **Placeholder Check** tool verifies that every interpolation placeholder
in the source survives, by count, into the target. A dropped `{count}` or an
unbalanced `<0>` is the kind of break that crashes a localized application at
runtime, so a placeholder present fewer times in the target than in the source
is flagged as critical. It is read-only: it annotates findings and never
rewrites content.

Placeholders are detected with a built-in pattern covering the common
interpolation styles — `{{name}}`, `${name}`, `%(name)s` (Python),
`%1$s` (positional), `%s` / `%d` / `%@` (printf / Objective-C),
`{name}` / `{0}` (ICU / .NET), and numbered tags such as `<0>…</0>`. A target
locale is required; blocks without a target for that locale are skipped.

flagExtra.description: Report placeholders present in the target but absent from the source.
flagExtra.help:
When enabled (the default), a placeholder that appears in the target but
not the source — usually a stray token — is reported as a major issue, in
addition to the always-critical report of placeholders dropped from the
target.

limitations: Detection is pattern-based and covers the common interpolation styles listed above; placeholder syntaxes outside that set are not recognised.
The check compares placeholder counts, not positions; a placeholder present the correct number of times but in the wrong order is not flagged.
Requires a configured target locale; blocks without a target for that locale are skipped.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks that have a target for the configured locale; other parts pass through unchanged.
Dropped placeholders are reported at critical severity; extra placeholders (when flagExtra is enabled) at major severity.

examples.title: Only report dropped placeholders
examples.description: Disable extra-placeholder reporting and flag only placeholders missing from the target.

entries.description: Merges adjacent inline codes in the source and target part of a text unit. Also where possible, moves leading and trailing codes of the source to inter-segment TextParts. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

schema.title: Post-segmentation Inline Codes Simplifier
schema.description: Merges adjacent inline codes in the source and target part of a text unit. Also where possible, moves leading and trailing codes of the source to inter-segment TextParts. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

removeLeadingTrailingCodes.title: Remove leading and trailing codes
removeLeadingTrailingCodes.description: Removes leading and trailing codes from the source and place them in the skeleton.

mergeCodes.title: Merge codes
mergeCodes.description: Merges adjacent codes (leading/trailing codes are removed first (if option is enabled)).

rules.title: Simplifier Rules
rules.description: Code simplifier rules in Okapi Code Simplifier Rule Format, applied after segmentation.

toolMeta.description: Merges adjacent inline codes in the source and target part of a text unit. Also where possible, moves leading and trailing codes of the source to inter-segment TextParts. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events.

doc.overview: This step simplifies inline codes in text units by merging adjacent codes and optionally moving leading/trailing codes out of segments into inter-segment TextParts. **It must be run after segmentation.** Original (un-merged) codes are preserved as `okp:merged` attributes in the generated XLIFF file, and trimmed codes are written outside `mrk` elements.

mergeCodes.help:
Joins adjacent inline codes within each segment into a single code. This simplifies the segment for translation by reducing the number of individual code placeholders.

Original (un-merged) codes are preserved as `okp:merged` attributes in the generated XLIFF file, allowing the merge to be reversed if needed.
notes: Does not apply to bi-lingual formats (XLIFF, TMX, TTX) where source and target codes may differ and must align by ID.

removeLeadingTrailingCodes.help:
Moves leading and trailing inline codes out of each segment and places them in inter-segment TextParts (outside `mrk` elements in XLIFF). This reduces noise for translators by keeping non-translatable codes outside the segment boundaries.

The codes are not deleted — they are repositioned in the skeleton/inter-segment structure so the final output document remains identical.
notes: Does not apply to bi-lingual formats (XLIFF, TMX, TTX) where source and target codes must align by ID.

limitations: Bi-lingual formats (XLIFF, TMX, TTX, etc.) will **not** have their codes simplified, because codes may differ between source and target and must align by ID.
The step must be placed in the pipeline **after** segmentation — it operates on linguistically segmented text units.
processingNotes: Original un-merged codes are saved as `okp:merged` attributes in the generated XLIFF file, enabling reversibility.
Trimmed (leading/trailing) codes are written outside `mrk` elements in the XLIFF output.
The step examines each linguistically distinct segment within a TextUnit independently.

examples.title: Simplify codes after segmentation
examples.description: Run after the Segmentation step to merge adjacent codes and move boundary codes out of each segment. Original codes are preserved as `okp:merged` attributes in the generated XLIFF, so the simplification is reversible on write-back.

examples.title: Merge only, leave boundary codes in the segment
examples.description: Collapse adjacent codes within each segment but keep leading and trailing codes inside the `mrk` element. Use this when only intra-segment clutter needs reducing. Note that bilingual inputs (XLIFF, TMX, TTX) are not simplified, since their source and target codes must align by ID.

entries.description: Set or modify key-value properties on blocks.

onlyTranslatable.title: Only Translatable
onlyTranslatable.description: Only set properties on translatable blocks

overwrite.title: Overwrite Existing
overwrite.description: Overwrite existing properties with the same key

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: Key-value pairs to set on each block

doc.overview:
The **Properties Set** tool writes a fixed set of key-value properties onto
blocks. Properties are metadata that travel with a block and can influence
downstream tools and writers. Use it to tag content — for example marking a
set of blocks with a workflow status or a routing hint.

At least one property must be configured. By default the tool overwrites
existing properties with the same key and applies only to translatable
blocks.

onlyTranslatable.description: Only set properties on translatable blocks.
onlyTranslatable.help:
When enabled, non-translatable blocks are skipped. Disable it to set
properties on every block regardless of translatability.

overwrite.description: Overwrite existing properties with the same key.
overwrite.help:
When enabled, a property whose key already exists on the block is
replaced. When disabled, existing properties are kept and only missing
keys are added.

properties.description: The key-value pairs to set on each block.
properties.help:
A map of property names to values. Every listed property is written onto
each processed block. At least one entry is required.
properties.values: object of string keys to string values (required)

examples.title: Tag blocks with a status
examples.description: Set a workflow property on every translatable block.

examples.title: Add metadata without overwriting
examples.description: Only add a key when the block does not already have it.

entries.description: Generate deterministic pseudo-translations for localization-readiness testing.

expansionPercent.title: Expansion Percent
expansionPercent.description: Extra padding percentage added to simulate translation expansion (0 = no padding)

prefix.title: Prefix
prefix.description: Characters prepended before each translated block

suffix.title: Suffix
suffix.description: Characters appended after each translated block

doc.overview:
The **Pseudo Translate** tool produces fake translations that exercise a
product's localization without a real translator. It replaces ASCII letters
with accented look-alikes, wraps each segment in marker characters, and can
pad the text to simulate the length growth of a real translation. The result
reveals hard-coded strings, clipped layouts, and encoding problems before
translation begins. A target language is required (the conventional pseudo
locale is `qps`).

Inline codes and placeholders are protected: text inside `{...}` markers and
non-text runs are passed through unchanged so the runtime can still substitute
parameters. The transform is deterministic, so the same input always yields
the same output.

expansionPercent.description: Extra padding to simulate translation expansion.
expansionPercent.help:
Adds padding characters proportional to the text length, so the output is
longer than the source — a common effect of real translation. A value of
0 adds no padding.

prefix.description: Characters prepended to each translated segment.
prefix.help:
A marker placed before the pseudo-translated text. The default shade
marker makes the segment boundary visible so untranslated, concatenated,
or clipped strings stand out.

suffix.description: Characters appended to each translated segment.
suffix.help: A marker placed after the pseudo-translated text, mirroring the prefix.

limitations: Only ASCII letters are accented; other characters and content inside `{...}` placeholder markers are passed through unchanged.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks with a non-empty source; other parts pass through unchanged.
Inline codes and placeholders are preserved; the prefix/suffix wrap the whole segment once.
With a session cache, already-pseudo-translated targets are reused and fresh results are written back.

examples.title: Standard pseudo-translation
examples.description: Pseudo-translate to the conventional qps locale.

examples.title: Simulate 30% expansion
examples.description: Pad output to surface layout clipping.

entries.description: Run a suite of rule-based quality checks comparing source and target text.

absoluteMaxCharLength.title: Absolute Maximum Characters
absoluteMaxCharLength.description: Maximum number of characters allowed in any target segment

checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.title: Check Absolute Maximum Length
checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.description: Flag target segments that exceed an absolute character count limit

checkCodeDifference.title: Check Code Differences
checkCodeDifference.description: Verify that target segments have the same inline codes as source segments

checkCorruptedCharacters.title: Check Corrupted Characters
checkCorruptedCharacters.description: Check for patterns indicating encoding corruption (e.g. UTF-8 opened as ISO-8859-1)

checkDoubleSpaces.title: Check Double Spaces
checkDoubleSpaces.description: Check for double spaces in target text

checkDoubledWord.title: Warn on Doubled Words
checkDoubledWord.description: Check for consecutive repeated words in target text

checkEmptySource.title: Warn on Non-Empty Target with Empty Source
checkEmptySource.description: Check for non-empty target when source is empty

checkEmptyTarget.title: Warn on Empty Target
checkEmptyTarget.description: Check for empty target when source has content

checkLeadingWhitespace.title: Check Leading Whitespace
checkLeadingWhitespace.description: Check for leading whitespace mismatches between source and target

checkMaxCharLength.title: Check Maximum Length Ratio
checkMaxCharLength.description: Flag targets longer than a percentage of source character length

checkMinCharLength.title: Check Minimum Length Ratio
checkMinCharLength.description: Flag targets shorter than a percentage of source character length

checkPatterns.title: Check Patterns
checkPatterns.description: Verify that source patterns have expected corresponding content in the target

checkSpanConstraints.title: Check Span Constraints
checkSpanConstraints.description: Check non-deletable and non-cloneable span constraint violations

checkTargetSameAsSource.title: Warn on Target Same as Source
checkTargetSameAsSource.description: Check when target text is identical to source text

checkTrailingWhitespace.title: Check Trailing Whitespace
checkTrailingWhitespace.description: Check for trailing whitespace mismatches between source and target

doubledWordExceptions.title: Doubled Word Exceptions
doubledWordExceptions.description: Semicolon-separated list of words allowed to repeat (e.g. sie;vous;nous)

maxCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for Long Text (Max)
maxCharLengthAbove.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for long text

maxCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for Short Text (Max)
maxCharLengthBelow.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for short text

maxCharLengthBreak.title: Short/Long Threshold (Max)
maxCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered long for the maximum length check

minCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for Long Text (Min)
minCharLengthAbove.description: Minimum required percentage of source length for long text

minCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for Short Text (Min)
minCharLengthBelow.description: Minimum required percentage of source length for short text

minCharLengthBreak.title: Short/Long Threshold (Min)
minCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered long for the minimum length check

strictCodeOrder.title: Enforce Strict Code Order
strictCodeOrder.description: Flag differences when codes appear in a different order between source and target

targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.title: Include Codes in Comparison
targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.description: Include inline codes when comparing source and target for identity

targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.title: Include Number-Only Segments
targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.description: Include number-only segments in same-as-source comparison

doc.overview:
The **QA Check** tool runs a battery of rule-based checks over each
translation and records the findings on the block's properties as a JSON array
of QA issues, along with a pass/fail flag. The checks span several categories:
general text issues (empty target, whitespace mismatches, double spaces,
doubled words, target identical to source, corrupted characters), inline-code
consistency, span constraint violations, regex pattern preservation, and length
ratios. A target locale is required; when a block has no translation, only the
empty-target check applies.

Every check can be toggled independently, and the length checks share the
long/short ratio model used by the dedicated length-check tool. This tool is
the rule-based counterpart to the LLM-based AI QA check: it is deterministic and
needs no credentials.

absoluteMaxCharLength.description: Maximum number of characters allowed in any target segment.
absoluteMaxCharLength.help: The absolute character cap used by the absolute-maximum-length check.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this limit to take effect

checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.description: Flag target segments that exceed an absolute character count limit.
checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.help:
When enabled, the target is flagged when it exceeds the absolute character
count given by `absoluteMaxCharLength`, independent of source length.

dependsOn.condition: sets the limit applied when this check is enabled

checkCodeDifference.description: Verify that the target has the same inline codes as the source.
checkCodeDifference.help:
Flags warnings when inline codes are missing from or extra in the target
compared with the source, counted by code type.

checkCorruptedCharacters.description: Check for patterns indicating encoding corruption.
checkCorruptedCharacters.help:
Flags a warning when the target contains characters that suggest encoding
corruption (for example UTF-8 misread as ISO-8859-1, signalled by the
Unicode replacement character).

checkDoubleSpaces.description: Check for double spaces in the target.
checkDoubleSpaces.help: Flags a warning when the target contains two consecutive spaces.

checkDoubledWord.description: Check for consecutive repeated words in the target.
checkDoubledWord.help:
Flags a warning when the same word appears twice in a row in the target,
unless the word is listed in `doubledWordExceptions`.

checkEmptySource.description: Warn when the target is non-empty but the source is empty.
checkEmptySource.help: Flags a warning when the target has content but the source is empty.

checkEmptyTarget.description: Warn when the target is empty but the source has content.
checkEmptyTarget.help:
Flags an error when a block has source content but no (or empty) target
text. This is the one check that still applies when a block has no
translation at all.

checkLeadingWhitespace.description: Check for leading-whitespace mismatches between source and target.
checkLeadingWhitespace.help:
Flags a warning when the leading whitespace of the target differs from that
of the source.

checkMaxCharLength.description: Flag targets longer than a percentage of source character length.
checkMaxCharLength.help:
Enables the maximum length-ratio check, using a long/short model: the
allowed percentage depends on whether the source is above or below the
maximum breakpoint.

checkMinCharLength.description: Flag targets shorter than a percentage of source character length.
checkMinCharLength.help:
Enables the minimum length-ratio check, using a long/short model: the
required percentage depends on whether the source is above or below the
minimum breakpoint.

checkPatterns.description: Verify that source patterns have expected corresponding content in the target.
checkPatterns.help:
When enabled, configured source/target regex pattern pairs are checked so
that patterns found in the source are preserved in the target. The pattern
pairs themselves are supplied programmatically.

checkSpanConstraints.description: Check non-deletable and non-cloneable span constraint violations.
checkSpanConstraints.help:
Flags an error when a non-deletable inline code is missing from the target,
or a non-cloneable inline code is duplicated in the target, according to the
per-code constraints carried on the source runs.

checkTargetSameAsSource.description: Warn when the target is identical to the source.
checkTargetSameAsSource.help:
Flags a warning when the target text equals the source text and contains at
least one letter or digit. Number-only segments are governed by
`targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers`.

checkTerminology.help: Enables terminology verification within QA check. Disabled by default.

checkTrailingWhitespace.description: Check for trailing-whitespace mismatches between source and target.
checkTrailingWhitespace.help:
Flags a warning when the trailing whitespace of the target differs from that
of the source.

doubledWordExceptions.help:
A semicolon-separated list of words that may legitimately appear twice in a
row (for example certain pronouns), so they are not flagged by the
doubled-word check.

dependsOn.condition: only consulted when the doubled-word check is enabled

maxCharLengthAbove.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for long text.
maxCharLengthAbove.help: The maximum target/source ratio applied when the source is long.

maxCharLengthBelow.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for short text.
maxCharLengthBelow.help: The maximum target/source ratio applied when the source is short.

maxCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered long (max check).
maxCharLengthBreak.help:
Source text longer than this is treated as long for the maximum-ratio check;
shorter text is treated as short.

minCharLengthAbove.description: Minimum required percentage of source length for long text.
minCharLengthAbove.help: The minimum target/source ratio applied when the source is long.

minCharLengthBelow.description: Minimum required percentage of source length for short text.
minCharLengthBelow.help: The minimum target/source ratio applied when the source is short.

minCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered long (min check).
minCharLengthBreak.help:
Source text longer than this is treated as long for the minimum-ratio check;
shorter text is treated as short.

strictCodeOrder.description: Flag differences when codes appear in a different order.
strictCodeOrder.help:
When enabled, and no count differences were found, the target is flagged if
its inline codes appear in a different order than in the source.

dependsOn.condition: the order check runs alongside code-difference checking

targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.description: Include inline codes when comparing source and target for identity.
targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.help:
Controls whether inline codes are taken into account in the
same-as-source comparison.

targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.description: Include number-only segments in the same-as-source comparison.
targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.help:
When enabled, segments containing only numbers (and punctuation) are
included in the target-same-as-source check. Disable it to ignore
number-only segments, which are often legitimately identical.

limitations: Length and ratio checks compare character lengths only when source and target are both non-empty.
The regex pattern pairs used by `checkPatterns` are supplied programmatically, not as schema parameters.
Requires a configured target locale.
processingNotes: Operates on translatable blocks; non-translatable structure passes through unchanged.
When a block has no translation, only the empty-target check applies.
Findings are stored on the block as a JSON array of QA issues with a pass/fail flag.

examples.title: Inline-code and whitespace checks only
examples.description: Focus on code preservation and whitespace, disabling the length-ratio checks.
examples.config:
checkCodeDifference: true
checkLeadingWhitespace: true
checkTrailingWhitespace: true
checkMaxCharLength: false
checkMinCharLength: false

examples.title: Enforce an absolute character cap
examples.description: Flag any target segment longer than 200 characters.

entries.description: Compare source and target for quality. Expects: filter events or raw documents. Sends back: filter events or raw document.

schema.title: Quality Check
schema.description: Configurable quality assurance checks for bilingual content: whitespace, inline codes, patterns, length, characters, terminology, and LanguageTool grammar verification.

checkCharacters.title: Check Against Charset Encoding
checkCharacters.description: Warn if a target character cannot be represented in the specified character set encoding.

checkAllowedCharacters.title: Check ITS Allowed Characters
checkAllowedCharacters.description: Verify target text against the ITS Allowed Characters annotation property.

charset.title: Character Set Encoding
charset.description: Name of the character set encoding to validate against (e.g. ISO-8859-1). Leave empty to use only the regex pattern below.

extraCharsAllowed.title: Extra Allowed Characters (Regex)
extraCharsAllowed.description: Regular expression matching additional characters to allow beyond the charset. These characters pass validation even if not in the charset.

corruptedCharacters.title: Check Corrupted Characters
corruptedCharacters.description: Check for special patterns that often indicate encoding corruption (e.g. UTF-8 file opened as ISO-8859-1).

leadingWS.title: Check Leading Whitespace
leadingWS.description: Flag text units where leading whitespace differs between source and target.

trailingWS.title: Check Trailing Whitespace
trailingWS.description: Flag text units where trailing whitespace differs between source and target.

emptyTarget.title: Warn on Empty Target
emptyTarget.description: Flag segments where the target is empty when the source is not empty.

emptySource.title: Warn on Non-Empty Target with Empty Source
emptySource.description: Flag segments where the target is not empty but the source is empty.

targetSameAsSource.title: Warn on Target Same as Source
targetSameAsSource.description: Flag segments where the target translation is identical to the source. Only checked when the source contains at least one word character.

targetSameAsSourceForSameLanguage.title: Verify for Same Language Family
targetSameAsSourceForSameLanguage.description: Also apply the target-same-as-source check when source and target are in the same language family (e.g. en vs. en-GB, or fr-FR vs. fr-CA).

targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.title: Include Codes in Comparison
targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.description: Include inline codes when comparing source and target for identity. If set and the only difference is a code, the segment is not flagged.

targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.title: Include Number-Only Segments
targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.description: Include segments containing only numbers in the same-as-source comparison.

checkXliffSchema.title: Validate XLIFF Schema
checkXliffSchema.description: Validate XLIFF input documents against an XLIFF schema.

doubledWord.title: Warn on Doubled Words
doubledWord.description: Flag target segments with consecutive repeated words (e.g. "is is" in "This is is an example"). Case-insensitive.

doubledWordExceptions.title: Doubled Word Exceptions
doubledWordExceptions.description: Semicolon-separated list of words that may be repeated (e.g. "vous;nous"). Case-insensitive.

codeDifference.title: Check Code Differences
codeDifference.description: Verify that target segments have the same inline codes as source segments. Reports both missing and extra codes.

guessOpenClose.title: Guess Opening/Closing Types
guessOpenClose.description: Try to infer whether placeholder codes are opening or closing when not explicitly marked.

strictCodeOrder.title: Enforce Strict Code Order
strictCodeOrder.description: Also flag differences when codes appear in a different order between source and target.

extraCodesAllowed.title: Extra Codes Allowed
extraCodesAllowed.description: List of inline code type names that are allowed to appear in the target even if not present in the source.

missingCodesAllowed.title: Missing Codes Allowed
missingCodesAllowed.description: List of inline code type names that are allowed to be missing from the target even if present in the source.

typesToIgnore.title: Code Types to Ignore
typesToIgnore.description: Semicolon-separated list of inline code types to skip during code verification (e.g. "mrk;x-df-s;").

checkWithLT.title: Use LanguageTool
checkWithLT.description: Run grammar and style checks using a LanguageTool server. Requires a running LanguageTool instance.

serverURL.title: Server URL
serverURL.description: URL of the LanguageTool server (e.g. http://localhost:8081/).

ltBilingualMode.title: Bilingual Mode
ltBilingualMode.description: Enable bilingual mode for LanguageTool verification.

checkStorageSize.title: Check ITS Storage Size
checkStorageSize.description: Warn if a source or target text unit does not fit its ITS Storage Size property.

checkMaxCharLength.title: Check Maximum Length Ratio
checkMaxCharLength.description: Flag targets longer than a given percentage of source character length. Different percentages apply to short and long text.

maxCharLengthBreak.title: Short/Long Threshold (Max)
maxCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered "long" for the maximum length check.

maxCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for Long Text (Max)
maxCharLengthAbove.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for text longer than the threshold.

maxCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for Short Text (Max)
maxCharLengthBelow.description: Maximum allowed percentage of source length for text at or below the threshold.

checkMinCharLength.title: Check Minimum Length Ratio
checkMinCharLength.description: Flag targets shorter than a given percentage of source character length. Different percentages apply to short and long text.

minCharLengthBreak.title: Short/Long Threshold (Min)
minCharLengthBreak.description: Character count above which text is considered "long" for the minimum length check.

minCharLengthAbove.title: Percentage for Long Text (Min)
minCharLengthAbove.description: Minimum required percentage of source length for text longer than the threshold.

minCharLengthBelow.title: Percentage for Short Text (Min)
minCharLengthBelow.description: Minimum required percentage of source length for text at or below the threshold.

checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.title: Check Absolute Maximum Length
checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.description: Flag target segments that exceed an absolute character count limit.

absoluteMaxCharLength.title: Absolute Maximum Characters
absoluteMaxCharLength.description: Maximum number of characters allowed in any target segment.

outputPath.title: Report File Path
outputPath.description: Path of the report file to generate. Use ${rootDir} for the project root directory.

outputType.title: Report Format
outputType.description: Format of the generated quality check report.

autoOpen.title: Open Report After Completion
autoOpen.description: Automatically open the report file in the default application after processing.

saveSession.title: Save Session
saveSession.description: Save the QA session to a file for later review in CheckMate.

sessionPath.title: Session File Path
sessionPath.description: Path for the saved QA session file.

scope.title: Processing Scope
scope.description: Which entries to process based on approval status. Non-translatable entries are always skipped.

showFullPath.title: Show Full Paths in Report
showFullPath.description: Display full file paths in the report instead of relative paths.

includeTextContainers.title: Include Text Containers
includeTextContainers.description: Include the source and target TextContainer objects with each reported issue. Off by default for performance.

useGenericCodes.title: Use Generic Codes in Display
useGenericCodes.description: Use generic code placeholders in the report display instead of original code data.

checkPatterns.title: Check Patterns
checkPatterns.description: Verify that source patterns defined in the list have their expected corresponding content in the target.

showOnlyPatternDescription.title: Show Pattern Description Only
showOnlyPatternDescription.description: When a pattern error is reported, show only the pattern description instead of full match details.

patterns.description: Regex pattern pairs for verifying source-target consistency. Each pattern defines a source regex, a target regex (or <same> to reuse the source match), severity, and matching direction.
patterns.title: Source/Target Pattern Rules

checkTerms.title: Verify Terminology
checkTerms.description: Verify terminology usage against a glossary file (TBX, CSV, or tab-delimited). Experimental feature.

termsPath.title: Glossary File Path
termsPath.description: Path to the glossary file (TBX, CSV, or tab-delimited TXT).

checkBlacklist.title: Check Blacklist
checkBlacklist.description: Check target text against a list of forbidden terms.

allowBlacklistSub.title: Detect Substrings
allowBlacklistSub.description: Allow substring matches when checking blacklisted terms (otherwise whole-word only).

blacklistSrc.title: Check Source Against Blacklist
blacklistSrc.description: Also check the source text against the blacklist, not just the target.

blacklistPath.title: Blacklist File Path
blacklistPath.description: Path to the tab-delimited file containing forbidden terms.

stringMode.title: Use String Matching
stringMode.description: Use string-based matching instead of word-boundary matching for terminology verification.

betweenCodes.title: Match Between Codes Only
betweenCodes.description: Strings must appear between inline codes to count as a terminology match.

toolMeta.description: Compare source and target for quality. Expects: filter events or raw documents. Sends back: filter events or raw document.

doc.overview: The Quality Check step compares source and target text units to generate a report of potential translation issues. It supports both filter events and raw document input, performing checks on whitespace, empty segments, inline codes, character patterns, length ratios, terminology, and more. When used with filter events, the report is generated at batch end; with raw documents, the session opens in CheckMate for interactive review.

absoluteMaxCharLength.description: Maximum number of characters allowed in any target segment.
absoluteMaxCharLength.help: The maximum number of characters allowed in any target segment when absolute max length checking is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

allowBlacklistSub.description: Allow substring matches when checking blacklisted terms.
allowBlacklistSub.help: Allows substring matching when checking blacklisted terms. When disabled, only whole-word matches are flagged.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

autoOpen.description: Automatically open the report file after processing.
autoOpen.help: Automatically opens the generated report file in the default browser/application after processing completes.

betweenCodes.description: Restrict terminology checking to text between inline codes.
betweenCodes.help: Restricts terminology checking to text between inline codes only.

blacklistPath.description: Path to the file containing forbidden terms.
blacklistPath.help: Path to the blacklist file containing forbidden terms.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

blacklistSrc.description: Also check the source text against the blacklist.
blacklistSrc.help: Also checks the source text against the blacklist, not just the target.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

charset.description: Charset encoding to validate target text against.
charset.help: The character set encoding to validate target text against. Only characters representable in this encoding are allowed. Can be left empty to use only the `extraCharsAllowed` regex.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.description: Flag targets exceeding an absolute character count limit.
checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength.help: Flags any target segment exceeding an absolute character count limit, regardless of source length.

dependsOn.condition: defines the limit when this is enabled

checkAllowedCharacters.description: Enable character-level validation of target text.
checkAllowedCharacters.help: Enables character-level validation of target text, checking against a charset encoding and/or a regex of allowed characters.

checkBlacklist.description: Check target text against a list of forbidden terms.
checkBlacklist.help: Enables checking target text against a blacklist of forbidden terms or expressions.

checkCharacters.description: Validate target characters against a specific charset encoding.
checkCharacters.help: Checks target characters against the specified `charset` encoding. Characters not representable in that encoding are flagged.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

checkMaxCharLength.description: Flag targets longer than a percentage of source character length.
checkMaxCharLength.help: Flags target text that is **longer** than a given percentage of the source character length. Uses separate thresholds for short and long text. Length is character count **excluding inline codes** and must be tuned per language pair.

checkMinCharLength.description: Flag targets shorter than a percentage of source character length.
checkMinCharLength.help: Flags target text that is **shorter** than a given percentage of the source character length. Uses separate thresholds for short and long text, same logic as the max length check.

checkPatterns.description: Verify that source patterns have expected corresponding content in the target.
checkPatterns.help: Enables verification that source patterns (defined in `sourcePatternN`/`targetPatternN` pairs) have their expected corresponding content in the target. Each pattern rule specifies a regex to find in one side and the expected match in the other.

checkStorageSize.description: Verify target fits within the storage size defined by the document format.
checkStorageSize.help: Verifies that the target fits within the storage size defined in the document format (e.g., `maxwidth` attribute in XLIFF).

checkTerms.description: Verify terminology using a glossary or termbase file.
checkTerms.help: Enables terminology verification using a glossary/termbase file. Source terms found in the termbase are checked against their expected target translations.

checkWithLT.description: Run additional grammar/style checks using a LanguageTool server.
checkWithLT.help: Runs additional verifications using a [LanguageTool](http://www.languagetool.org) server. Requires a running LanguageTool instance accessible at the configured `serverURL`.
notes: Using LanguageTool may **significantly increase** processing time.
You must have a LanguageTool server running before enabling this option.

checkXliffSchema.description: Validate input against an XLIFF schema.
checkXliffSchema.help: Validates the input against an XLIFF schema. Use `xliffSchemaType` to choose between Transitional and Strict validation modes.

codeDifference.description: Verify that target segments have the same inline codes as source segments.
codeDifference.help: Verifies that the target content has the same inline codes as the source. Both **missing codes** (in source but not target) and **extra codes** (in target but not source) are reported. A difference only in code order does **not** trigger a warning.
notes: The `extraCodesAllowed` and `missingCodesAllowed` parameters can whitelist specific codes.

corruptedCharacters.description: Detect patterns indicating encoding corruption in target text.
corruptedCharacters.help: Checks for special character patterns that often indicate encoding corruption — for example, a UTF-8 file opened as ISO-8859-1. Does not detect all possible corruption, only common patterns.

descPattern0.help: Short human-readable description of what this pattern rule checks. Shown in the report.

doubledWord.description: Flag target segments with consecutive repeated words.
doubledWord.help: Flags target segments containing sequential repetitions of the same word, e.g., "is is" in "this is is an example". The check is **case-insensitive** — "This this" is also flagged.
examples: `this is is an example` — flagged ('is is')

doubledWordExceptions.description: Semicolon-separated words allowed to be repeated.
doubledWordExceptions.help: Semicolon-separated list of words that are allowed to be repeated without triggering a doubled-word warning. Exceptions are **case-insensitive**.
doubledWordExceptions.values: Words separated by semicolons with **no spaces** around the delimiter.
examples: `sie;vous;nous` — allows 'vous vous' and 'nous nous' in French, 'sie sie' in German

dependsOn.condition: must be true

emptySource.description: Flag segments where the source is empty but the target is not.
emptySource.help: Flags segments where the source is empty but the target contains content. May indicate orphaned or misaligned translations.

emptyTarget.help: Flags segments where the target translation is empty but the source contains content. This is a common indicator of untranslated segments.

extraCharsAllowed.description: Regex matching additional characters to allow beyond the charset.
extraCharsAllowed.help: A regex matching additional characters to allow beyond the specified charset. These characters pass validation even if not in the charset.
extraCharsAllowed.values: Java regular expression matching allowed characters. Leave empty to not use.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

extraCodesAllowed.description: Number of extra inline codes allowed in target without warning.
extraCodesAllowed.help: Number of inline codes allowed to be extra in the target (present in target but not source) without triggering a warning.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

fromSourcePattern0.description: Search source first then verify target (true) or vice versa (false).
fromSourcePattern0.help: When `true`, the source pattern is searched first; if found, the target pattern is verified in the target. When `false`, the target pattern is searched first, then the source is checked — useful for detecting **extra** patterns in the target.

fromSourcePattern1.description: Search source first then verify target (true) or vice versa (false).
fromSourcePattern1.help: Direction for pattern rule #1. `true` = source→target, `false` = target→source.

fromSourcePattern2.description: Search source first then verify target (true) or vice versa (false).

fromSourcePattern3.description: Search source first then verify target (true) or vice versa (false).

fromSourcePattern4.description: Search source first then verify target (true) or vice versa (false).

fromSourcePattern5.description: Search source first then verify target (true) or vice versa (false).

fromSourcePattern6.description: Search source first then verify target (true) or vice versa (false).

fromSourcePattern7.description: Search source first then verify target (true) or vice versa (false).

guessOpenClose.description: Infer whether inline codes are opening or closing when not explicitly marked.
guessOpenClose.help: Attempts to infer whether inline codes are opening or closing tags when this information is not explicitly available in the format. Improves accuracy of code-difference checks.

includeTextContainers.description: Include non-segment text parts (inter-segment content) in quality checks.
includeTextContainers.help: Controls whether text containers (non-segment parts between segments in a text unit) are included in the verification scope.

leadingWS.help: Compares leading whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines at the start) between source and target text units. Differences are flagged as issues in the report.

ltBilingualMode.description: Auto-translate LanguageTool error messages into another language.
ltBilingualMode.help: When enabled, auto-translates LanguageTool error messages from the target language into another language. Useful when LanguageTool returns messages in the verified language (e.g., Polish errors for Polish text) but you need them in English.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

maxCharLengthAbove.description: Character count above which text is considered long for max length check.
maxCharLengthAbove.help: Character count threshold that divides text into "short" and "long" categories for the maximum length check. Text with this many characters or fewer uses the short-text percentage.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

maxCharLengthBelow.description: Maximum length percentage for long text.
maxCharLengthBelow.help: The percentage threshold for maximum length of **long** text (source length > `maxCharLengthAbove`).

dependsOn.condition: must be true

maxCharLengthBreak.description: Maximum length percentage for short text.
maxCharLengthBreak.help: The percentage threshold for maximum length of **short** text (source length ≤ `maxCharLengthAbove`). Target exceeding this percentage of source length is flagged.
maxCharLengthBreak.values: Percentage value (e.g., `20` means target can be at most 20% longer than source for short texts).

dependsOn.condition: must be true

minCharLengthAbove.description: Character count above which text is considered long for min length check.
minCharLengthAbove.help: Character count threshold dividing text into short/long for the minimum length check.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

minCharLengthBelow.description: Minimum length percentage for long text.
minCharLengthBelow.help: The percentage threshold for minimum length of **long** text (source length > `minCharLengthAbove`).

dependsOn.condition: must be true

minCharLengthBreak.description: Minimum length percentage for short text.
minCharLengthBreak.help: The percentage threshold for minimum length of **short** text (source length ≤ `minCharLengthAbove`).

dependsOn.condition: must be true

missingCodesAllowed.description: Number of missing inline codes allowed from target without warning.
missingCodesAllowed.help: Number of inline codes allowed to be missing from the target (present in source but not target) without triggering a warning.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

outputPath.description: Path of the HTML report file to generate.
outputPath.help: Full path to the HTML report file generated after processing. Supports the `${rootDir}` variable to resolve relative to the input root directory.
outputPath.values: File path string. Use `${rootDir}` for the root directory of the input batch.

outputType.description: Format of the generated quality check report.
outputType.help: Selects the format of the generated report.
outputType.values: `0` = HTML report, other values for alternative output formats.

patternCount.help: The number of pattern rules defined. Controls how many `usePatternN`/`sourcePatternN`/`targetPatternN` sets are active.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

saveSession.description: Save the QA session to a file for later review in CheckMate.
saveSession.help: Saves the QA session to a `.qcs` file so it can be reopened later in CheckMate for further review.

scope.description: Which entries to process: all, approved only, or non-approved only.
scope.help: Controls which entries are processed based on their *Approved* property. Non-translatable entries are **never** processed regardless of this setting.
scope.values: `0` = all entries, `1` = only approved entries, `2` = only non-approved entries.

serverURL.description: URL of the LanguageTool server instance.
serverURL.help: URL of the LanguageTool server instance to connect to for grammar/style checking.
serverURL.values: HTTP URL, typically `http://localhost:8081/` for a local server.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

sessionPath.description: Path for the saved QA session file.
sessionPath.help: Path for the saved QA session file. Supports `${rootDir}` variable.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

severityPattern0.description: Warning severity level for pattern rule #0.
severityPattern0.help: The severity level assigned to warnings from this pattern rule.

severityPattern1.description: Warning severity level for pattern rule #1.

severityPattern2.description: Warning severity level for pattern rule #2.

severityPattern3.description: Warning severity level for pattern rule #3.

severityPattern4.description: Warning severity level for pattern rule #4.

severityPattern5.description: Warning severity level for pattern rule #5.

severityPattern6.description: Warning severity level for pattern rule #6.

severityPattern7.description: Warning severity level for pattern rule #7.

showFullPath.description: Display full file paths in the report instead of filenames only.
showFullPath.help: Displays the full file path in the report output rather than just the filename.

showOnlyPatternDescription.description: Show only the pattern description in reports instead of full match details.
showOnlyPatternDescription.help: In the report, shows only the pattern description instead of the full matching details for pattern-based checks.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

singlePattern0.description: Expect only a single occurrence for pattern #0.
singlePattern0.help: When enabled, only a single occurrence of the pattern is expected (rather than matching count between source and target).

singlePattern1.description: Expect only a single occurrence for pattern #1.
singlePattern1.help: When enabled, only a single occurrence is expected for pattern rule #1.

singlePattern2.description: Expect only a single occurrence for pattern #2.
singlePattern2.help: When enabled, only a single occurrence is expected for pattern rule #2.

singlePattern3.description: Expect only a single occurrence for pattern #3.
singlePattern3.help: When enabled, only a single occurrence is expected for pattern rule #3.

singlePattern4.description: Expect only a single occurrence for pattern #4.
singlePattern4.help: When enabled, only a single occurrence is expected for pattern rule #4.

singlePattern5.description: Expect only a single occurrence for pattern #5.
singlePattern5.help: When enabled, only a single occurrence is expected for pattern rule #5.

singlePattern6.description: Expect only a single occurrence for pattern #6.
singlePattern6.help: When enabled, only a single occurrence is expected for pattern rule #6.

singlePattern7.description: Expect only a single occurrence for pattern #7.
singlePattern7.help: When enabled, only a single occurrence is expected for pattern rule #7.

sourcePattern0.description: Regular expression to match in source for pattern #0.
sourcePattern0.help: Java regular expression to search for in the source text for pattern rule #0.

sourcePattern1.description: Regular expression to match in source for pattern #1.

sourcePattern2.description: Regular expression to match in source for pattern #2.

sourcePattern3.description: Regular expression to match in source for pattern #3.

sourcePattern4.description: Regular expression to match in source for pattern #4.

sourcePattern5.description: Regular expression to match in source for pattern #5.

sourcePattern6.description: Regular expression to match in source for pattern #6.

sourcePattern7.description: Regular expression to match in source for pattern #7.

strictCodeOrder.description: Also flag code differences when codes appear in a different order.
strictCodeOrder.help: When enabled, the code difference check also flags cases where codes appear in a different order between source and target. By default, only missing/extra codes are flagged, not reordering.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this to have effect

stringMode.description: Use string-based matching instead of word-boundary matching for terms.
stringMode.help: Uses string-based comparison for terminology matching instead of word-boundary-aware matching.

targetPattern0.description: Expected pattern in target for pattern #0, or <same>.
targetPattern0.help: Expected pattern in the target text. Use `<same>` if the target should contain the exact same match as found in the source.
targetPattern0.values: Java regex, or the keyword `<same>` to expect an identical match.

targetPattern1.description: Expected pattern in target for pattern #1, or <same>.
targetPattern1.help: Target regex for pattern rule #1. Use `<same>` for identical match.

targetPattern2.description: Expected pattern in target for pattern #2, or <same>.
targetPattern2.help: Target regex for pattern rule #2. Use `<same>` for identical match.

targetPattern3.description: Expected pattern in target for pattern #3, or <same>.
targetPattern3.help: Target regex for pattern rule #3. Use `<same>` for identical match.

targetPattern4.description: Expected pattern in target for pattern #4, or <same>.
targetPattern4.help: Target regex for pattern rule #4. Use `<same>` for identical match.

targetPattern5.description: Expected pattern in target for pattern #5, or <same>.
targetPattern5.help: Target regex for pattern rule #5. Use `<same>` for identical match.

targetPattern6.description: Expected pattern in target for pattern #6, or <same>.
targetPattern6.help: Target regex for pattern rule #6. Use `<same>` for identical match.

targetPattern7.description: Expected pattern in target for pattern #7, or <same>.
targetPattern7.help: Target regex for pattern rule #7. Use `<same>` for identical match.

targetSameAsSource.description: Flag segments where the target translation is identical to the source.
targetSameAsSource.help:
Flags segments where the target text is identical to the source text. Only checked when the source contains at least one word character matching `[\p{Ll}\p{Lu}\p{Lt}\p{Lo}\p{Nd}]` (any Unicode letter or digit). Inline codes are excluded from comparison.

When a match is found, the tool cross-references against patterns with `<same>` as the expected target — if the text matches such a pattern, no warning is generated since the target is expected to be identical.
notes: Entries like `%s : %d` (only codes and punctuation) are skipped because they contain no word characters.
Entries like `-------- S= %d` are checked because 'S' is a word character.

targetSameAsSourceForSameLanguage.description: Apply the target-same-as-source check even when source and target languages match.
targetSameAsSourceForSameLanguage.help: Controls the target-same-as-source check behavior when source and target languages are identical. Enable to still flag identical segments even for same-language pairs.

targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.description: Include inline codes when comparing source and target for identity.
targetSameAsSourceWithCodes.help: Includes inline codes in the source-vs-target identity comparison. When enabled, if the only difference between source and target is an inline code, the segment is **not** flagged (because the codes make them technically different).

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this to have effect

targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.description: Include numeric values when comparing source and target for identity.
targetSameAsSourceWithNumbers.help: Includes numeric values in the source-vs-target identity comparison. When enabled, differences in numbers between source and target prevent the 'same as source' warning.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this to have effect

termsPath.description: Path to the termbase file for terminology verification.
termsPath.help: Path to the termbase file used for terminology verification.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

trailingWS.help: Compares trailing whitespace between source and target text units. Differences are flagged as issues in the report.

typesToIgnore.description: Semicolon-separated list of inline code types to skip during verification.
typesToIgnore.help: Semicolon-separated list of inline code types to ignore during code-difference verification.
typesToIgnore.values: Code type names separated by semicolons, e.g., `mrk;x-df-s;`.

useGenericCodes.description: Replace specific inline code content with generic placeholders before comparison.
useGenericCodes.help: Replaces specific inline code content with generic placeholders before comparison. Useful when code content varies but structure should match.

xliffSchemaType.description: XLIFF schema validation level: Transitional or Strict.
xliffSchemaType.help: Selects the XLIFF schema validation strictness level.
xliffSchemaType.values: `Transitional` for lenient validation, `Strict` for full schema compliance.

dependsOn.condition: must be true

limitations: No checks are performed on entries flagged as non-translatable.
Corrupted character detection only catches common patterns — not all possible encoding issues.
LanguageTool integration may significantly increase processing time.
Length checks use character count excluding inline codes and must be tuned per language pair.
processingNotes: With filter events input: events pass through to the next step; the report is generated at batch end with no interactive UI.
With raw documents input: documents are added to a CheckMate session; at batch end CheckMate opens for interactive review.
The step shares its parameter module with CheckMate — configuration is interchangeable.
A *text unit* may contain multiple *segments*; some checks operate at the text-unit level (whitespace) while others at the segment level (empty target, same as source).
When target-same-as-source is detected, patterns with `<same>` as expected target are cross-referenced — matching patterns suppress the warning.

examples.title: Basic QA with default patterns
examples.description: Run quality checks with default settings — whitespace, empty segments, code differences, and the 8 built-in pattern rules (parentheses, brackets, emails, URLs, IPs, printf codes, tripled letters).
examples.config:
```yaml
outputPath: ${rootDir}/qa-report.html
autoOpen: true
checkPatterns: true
codeDifference: true
doubledWord: true
```

examples.title: Length-focused QA for UI strings
examples.description: Check for target translations that are too long for UI display, using strict absolute max and percentage-based checks.
examples.config:
```yaml
checkMaxCharLength: true
maxCharLengthBreak: 30
maxCharLengthAbove: 50
maxCharLengthBelow: 20
checkAbsoluteMaxCharLength: true
absoluteMaxCharLength: 128
checkMinCharLength: false
```

examples.title: Pattern with <same> keyword
examples.description: The `<same>` keyword in a target pattern means the exact match found in the source must also appear in the target. This is the default for URLs, emails, and IP addresses.
examples.config:
```yaml
usePattern4: true
fromSourcePattern4: true
sourcePattern4: "https?:[\\w/\\.:;+\\-~\\%#\\$?=&,()]+"
targetPattern4: "<same>"
descPattern4: "URLs"
severityPattern4: 1
```

entries.description: Generates a Rainbow translation kit for a batch of input documents. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events or raw document

schema.title: Rainbow Translation Kit Creation
schema.description: Generates a Rainbow translation kit for a batch of input documents. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events or raw document

writerClass.title: Package Format
writerClass.description: Java class of the package writer that determines the translation kit output format (e.g., XLIFF, PO, OmegaT).

writerOptions.title: Writer Options
writerOptions.description: Package writer-specific options controlling the selected output format.

packageName.title: Package Name
packageName.description: Name of the package directory to create inside the output root.

packageDirectory.title: Output Root Directory
packageDirectory.description: Root directory where the translation package will be created; supports ${inputRootDir} and locale variables.

message.title: Package Message
message.description: Optional message or instructions to include with the translation package.

outputManifest.title: Output Manifest
outputManifest.description: Generate a manifest file required for merging the translated kit back into the original format.

createZip.title: Create ZIP Package
createZip.description: Create a ZIP archive (.rkp) of the generated translation package.

sendOutput.title: Send Output to Next Step
sendOutput.description: Send prepared files as raw documents to the next pipeline step instead of the original filter events.

supportFiles.title: Support Material Files
supportFiles.description: Additional files to include in the package as support material for translators.

toolMeta.description: Generates a Rainbow translation kit for a batch of input documents. Expects: filter events. Sends back: filter events or raw document

doc.overview: Creates a translation package (Rainbow translation kit) from a set of input documents processed through Okapi filters. Supports multiple package formats including Generic XLIFF, PO, OmegaT, XLIFF 2.0, and others. The extracted files can be merged back after translation using the Rainbow Translation Kit Merging Step.

createZip.description: Create a ZIP archive (.rkp) of the generated translation package.
createZip.help: Creates a ZIP archive of the generated package. The ZIP file is placed in the same directory as the package with a `.rkp` extension and the same name as the package.
examples: A package named `pack1` produces `pack1.rkp` in the output directory

message.description: Optional message or instructions to include with the translation package.
